gaps.title: The Compromise zone is a 'nobody won' default, not a positive-sum bargain — and the system itself admits the bargain game is undesigned
gaps.research_basis: Politics companion Part D.4: 'game' holds together four things a procedure can do — produce a winner, STRIKE A BARGAIN (positive-sum), discern a truth, enact a unity. Part 0's game-theoretic sense is often MIXED-MOTIVE / positive-sum. The interesting questions live in the gaps between these.
gaps.system_state: Everything resolves on one zero-sum 0-10 track; the 4-6 band is labeled 'Compromise' but yields no Domain Echo, no Obligation, and merely defers the same fight to a Chain Contest that starts at the prior track position.

locations.quote: Compromise: no Domain Echo.
When a contest ends in Compromise (Persuasion Track 4–6), the tension is deferred, not resolved.
Negotiation compromise resolution (ZOPA-style) | Identified as structurally different from Persuasion Track, not designed

gaps.critique: A genuine bargain is positive-sum: both sides give on a low-value axis to win a high-value one, and BOTH walk away with something they preferred to no-deal. Valoria's Compromise is the arithmetic midpoint of a zero-sum tug-of-war — a draw, explicitly stripped of consequence (no Domain Echo) and routed to a re-match. The spec itself flags that real compromise is 'structurally different from Persuasion Track' and was never built. So the one track cannot represent the second of the four games at all; it can only represent failure-to-win for both parties.
gaps.churn_relevance: A draw that produces 'no Domain Echo' is the inert outcome the churn axiom warns against — the only churn it generates is a Chain Contest to re-roll the SAME tug-of-war (up to 3 times, then a 4-season freeze). A real bargain generates far richer churn: the SHAPE of the trade (who conceded which axis) seeds future grievance, leverage, and obligation. The current design converts the most common contest outcome into the least generative one.
gaps.improvement: Make the Compromise band a real ZOPA without a new subsystem. Reuse the existing stakes ledger (§2 Step 6 already records 'what each side wins, loses, or compromises on') as a multi-axis stake vector; when the track lands 4-6, resolve as a TRADE: each side keeps the axis it scored higher on across exchanges (genre-won tracking already exists per §6 'Thread co-movement by winning genre'). Emit a Compromise Obligation (a bilateral two-clock pair via clock_registry, mirroring §6.1) rather than no Domain Echo. Both clocks are real churn; the asymmetry of the trade becomes the next arc's grievance.

verdict.reasoning: The core claim verifies: all three cited quotes are exact (§6 line 232 "Compromise: no Domain Echo"; §6.3 line 298 "the tension is deferred, not resolved"; §12 line 603 ZOPA "Identified as structurally different from Persuasion Track, not designed"). The sim corroborates that Compromise is mechanically a null: contest.py line 205 sets winner=None at track 4-6 with no axis/trade computation, and PERSUASION thresholds treat 4-6 as pure midpoint. The §2 Step 6 stakes ("what each side wins, loses, or compromises on", line 92; infill line 24) is a GM narrative note, NOT a multi-axis stake vector — so the finding is right that the engine has no positive-sum trade primitive, and no negotiation/ZOPA doc exists anywhere in the corpus. The second of the four 'games' (strike a bargain) genuinely is not represented as a designed outcome on the track. HOWEVER, the severity/leverage is overstated on two counts. (1) The claim that Compromise is universally 'the least generative outcome' and produces 'no churn but a re-roll' is false for the one place a contest stake is naturally divisible: §7 Succession (lines 341-355) already resolves Compromise as a track-weighted FACTION SPLIT (Track 4 = 60/40, Track 5 = 55/45, Track 6 = 50/50, per ED-762), with proportional Mandate/Wealth/Military/Influence/Stability inheritance, territory division, and a Stability-3 floor that explicitly preserves 'post-split civil-war drama' as playable churn. That is exactly the finding's prescription — a divided-stakes outcome whose asymmetry seeds the next arc's grievance — already built where it most applies. So Compromise is not uniformly inert; the succession resolver is a working precedent for the proposed mechanic. (2) For Formal/Grand contests the Compromise band is genuinely thin, but it is not consequence-free: it forces a Scene Slate Priority-1 crisis entry (§6.3), carries Disposition and Scar state forward, and can terminate in a 4-season cold-equilibrium freeze — which is churn of a different shape than the finding allows. Net: the gap (no positive-sum bargain primitive on the general track) is real, but 'converts the most common outcome into the least generative one' overstates it given §7's existing split logic, so leverage drops to medium.
verdict.evidence_quote: | Compromise (4-6) | **Faction split**: factional sub-divisions emerge ... New sub-faction acquires partial inheritance of original faction's stat values per §7.2.1 split-rule below; track-distance weighting applies (Track 4 = 60/40 majority-favored split; Track 5 = 55/45; Track 6 = 50/50 even split) per stress-test ED-762.
verdict.refined_improvement: Do NOT add a new ZOPA subsystem. Generalize the mechanic that §7.2.1 already proves: the track-distance-weighted split. Where a contest's §2 Step 6 stakes name more than one divisible axis, treat the 4-6 band as a proportional division of those stakes rather than a null — reusing the existing 60/40 / 55/45 / 50/50 weighting keyed to track position, and the existing genre-won attribution (§6 Thread co-movement) to decide WHICH side keeps which axis. Bind the result through the systems already on the bus: emit the asymmetric trade as a bilateral Obligation pair via clock_registry (the §6.1 Obligation primitive already supports settlement- and condition-targeted commitments), not a new echo type. This keeps it inside the sigma resolver + Key-bus discipline: Compromise stops being winner=None and becomes a two-clock partition whose imbalance is the next arc's leverage. Scope it as 'lift §7's split into the general track for multi-axis stakes', not 'design negotiation from scratch' — which is why effort is medium, not high.

gaps.title: The engine rewards win-at-all-costs and has no merit-channel — inverting pragma-dialectics' core finding
gaps.research_basis: Part VI.2-3 (pragma-dialectics): the healthiest deliberation is resolution-ON-THE-MERITS; 'trying to win at all costs' is the very disposition that PRODUCES fallacies; eristic (pure contest) is the DEGENERATE type, not the model type.
gaps.system_state: Obscuring/Suppression/Insinuation styles and Doubt Markers are pure positional weapons; winning by foreclosing the opponent's epistemic standing (Obscuring) is mechanically equivalent to — and sometimes better than — winning on substance, and nothing tracks or penalizes bad-faith play.

locations.quote: | Suppression | Memory + Obscuring | Burying inconvenient history |
Obscuring in CROSS: if the side with larger movement used Obscuring, place a Doubt Marker on the opponent instead of track movement.
Church boosts Obscuring — the Inquisitor's arguments that foreclose the accused's epistemic standing carry institutional weight.

gaps.critique: The system encodes the eristic disposition as a first-class, faction-boosted strategy with zero merit cost: burying history (Suppression) and implying unstated consequences (Insinuation) get the same +1D audience boosts as Precedent and Vision, and Obscuring wins place a Doubt Marker that handicaps the opponent's NEXT argument regardless of its truth. There is no variable that distinguishes 'won because right' from 'won because nastier.' Pragma-dialectics says this is precisely the degenerate mode; Valoria has made it the default skill expression.
gaps.churn_relevance: This is a subtle churn LOSS disguised as churn. Yes, machination generates story — but undifferentiated machination means every contest reads the same (apply maximum positional pressure). A merit/eristic distinction would make HOW you won a durable, queryable fact that seeds reputation, NPC trust, and later fallacy-exposure scenes — strictly more emergent narrative than a flat Doubt Marker.
gaps.improvement: Add a single per-side scalar, Standing-on-the-Merits, computed by the armature dot-product the engine already runs: when an argument's style matches the audience's BOOSTED axis AND addresses a known Belief (Revealing genres), +1; Obscuring wins that place Doubt Markers, -1. It is not a new track — it is a tag on the existing exchange ledger that, at post-contest, emits a Key: high merit → winner Renown/Disposition bonus; net-eristic win → a latent 'fallacy exposed' hook (a fieldwork/Appraise can later reveal it, reusing §6.2 Scar-visibility plumbing). Costs one accumulator, reuses the dot-product and the Key bus.

verdict.evidence_quote: An NPC revises a Belief when ALL of the following hold: 1. A Contest produces decisive outcome (Piety Track ≥ 7 or ≤ 3) against the NPC 2. The winning argument used the NPC's primary or secondary Resonant Style
verdict.reasoning:
The finding's central claim — "there is no variable that distinguishes 'won because right' from 'won because nastier'," and Obscuring is "mechanically equivalent to or better than winning on substance" — is substantially refuted by an existing, load-bearing mechanism the finding never examines: the Resonant Style / Belief Revision / Conviction Scar gradient.

WHERE IT LIVES: A bare Persuasion Track win only moves a clock and (per social_contest §6 RESOLUTION) clears its strain/Doubt Markers at scene end — ephemeral. The DURABLE, queryable consequences the finding asks for (reputation, NPC framework change, later scene seeds) are gated on substance, not positional pressure:
  - npc_behavior_v30 §3.2 Belief Revision (quoted): fires ONLY when "the winning argument used the NPC's primary or secondary Resonant Style." The RS taxonomy (§1.3) maps three of four styles — Evidence, Consequence, Solidarity — to REVEALING; only Authority maps to an Obscuring (Suppression) style. So a win driven by pure Obscuring/Suppression that does NOT match the NPC's RS and address a known Belief yields a track win with NO Belief revision and NO Scar — the framework is untouched.
  - npc_behavior_v30 §6.3 Targeting Effects: confirmation "requires the argument's content specifically addresses a known Belief," and Revealing genres carry the heavier durable payloads (Evidence: +Piety Track bypassing resistance; Consequence: +2 strain to thinnest defense; Solidarity: Belief-revision Ob −1). Obscuring's Authority targeting yields only a Doubt Marker.
  - The §6.2 Conviction Scar Visibility signal ("Something shifted in [NPC]'s expression") and the contest.py post-contest hook ("may trigger Conviction Scar via post-contest hook ... if a Conviction is named in stakes") are exactly the durable, later-revealable plumbing the improvement proposes to "reuse" — they already exist and already key off substantive (RS-matched, Belief-addressing) wins.

So the engine DOES distinguish won-by-substance (durable: Belief Revision, Scar, the §6.2 signal) from won-by-pressure (ephemeral: Doubt Marker / track movement). The distillation report itself lists "the Appraise-first contest" and the dual-armature/Belief machinery among the non-cuttable primitives — the merit channel is structural, not absent.

GRAIN OF TRUTH (why overstated, not refuted): the merit channel exists as a REWARD GRADIENT (substance unlocks durable effects; nastiness unlocks only ephemeral ones) but NOT as a symmetric ERISTIC COST. There is no latent "fallacy exposed" hook or Renown/Disposition penalty keyed to a net-eristic win. The finding misreads the missing eristic-penalty scalar as the absence of any merit channel. That is a real but narrow gap, and leverage is medium not high.

CITED-QUOTE CHECK: all three cited quotes locate verbatim (params/contest.md L40 Suppression row; social_contest_v30.md §4 L170 Obscuring-in-CROSS; §7 L324 "Church boosts Obscuring"). They are accurate but only describe the positional-weapon layer; the finding's error is stopping there and not reading the npc_behavior RS/Belief layer that supplies the merit differentiation.
verdict.refined_improvement: Drop the positive half of the proposal — "+1 when style matches the BOOSTED axis AND addresses a known Belief" simply re-derives the existing RS-targeting confirmation (npc_behavior §6.3) and Belief Revision gate (§3.2), so it is redundant and risks a parallel subsystem. Keep ONLY the novel negative half: when a decisive win is achieved WITHOUT triggering Belief Revision (i.e. the §3.2 conjunction failed — no RS match / no known Belief addressed) AND the winning side used an Obscuring style that placed a Doubt Marker, emit a latent "fallacy exposed" Key on the existing Key bus, surfaced later via the §6.2 Scar-visibility / Appraise-revelation plumbing (a fieldwork/Appraise can expose the bad-faith win). This is a single tag on the post-contest hook the contest.py already runs, derived from data the engine already computes (RS-match boolean + Obscuring-Doubt-Marker flag) — no new accumulator, no new dot-product call. It turns the eristic-cost gap into a one-line consequence on the existing reward gradient rather than a new merit track.

gaps.title: Inquiry ('discern a truth') is modeled only as a weapon — the loser can never be persuaded on the merits, despite the substrate already having a belief_revised Key
gaps.research_basis: Politics Part D.4: a procedure can DISCERN A TRUTH (inquiry), distinct from producing a winner. Part VI: resolution-on-the-merits is the healthy type — a participant genuinely changing their mind is the success state, not a casualty.
gaps.system_state: The only contest path to changed conviction is the Conviction Scar — a wound the WINNER inflicts on the loser via Resonant Style targeting; there is no resolution mode where a party is legitimately convinced and updates a Belief as a non-damaging outcome.

locations.quote: When a player's contest argument produces a Conviction Scar on an NPC ... 'Your argument reached something they cannot dismiss.'
+#10 belief_revised added 2026-05-02 per Stage 10 §4.1 finding
utterances tagged [INSTRUMENTAL] trigger the gate before the utterance resolves. Belief-gated utterances are tagged [SINCERE] and bypass the gate.

gaps.critique: The corpus ALREADY contains the two primitives inquiry needs: a state.belief_revised Key (articulation §6.3) and a Sincerity Gate that distinguishes sincere from instrumental speech (investigation_systems). Neither is wired into the contest. So 'I was persuaded' is representable everywhere EXCEPT the system whose entire job is persuasion — and inside the contest the closest analog (the Scar) is framed as structural damage 'they cannot dismiss,' i.e. coercion, not conviction. The truth-discernment game is not missing from the engine; it is missing from the contest's resolution table.
gaps.churn_relevance: A loser who is genuinely persuaded is a high-value churn event the design currently cannot produce on the merits — it converts an opponent, realigns a Stance Triangle, and emits belief_revised (which articulation §3.1 already routes to cut-scenes). Right now that conversion can only happen as a wound, which biases all emergent narrative toward grievance and away from the rarer, dramatically potent 'you changed my mind' beat.
gaps.improvement: Add an Inquiry resolution branch reusing existing primitives: when a Decisive win is achieved with Revealing-only styles (no Obscuring used) AND the winning argument targeted the loser's primary Resonant Style on the merits (Evidence/Consequence/Solidarity, already specified in contest_extensions), emit state.belief_revised on the LOSER instead of (or alongside) Contest Fatigue, and route the loser's Conviction via the existing Self-Other / axis shift rather than a Scar. Gate sincerity with the existing Spirit TN7 Ob1 Sincerity Gate so an instrumental win cannot fake a conversion. No new resolver — it is a post-contest Key emission keyed off styles already tracked.

verdict.evidence_quote: An NPC revises a Belief when ALL of the following hold: 1. A Contest produces decisive outcome (Piety Track >= 7 or <= 3) against the NPC 2. The winning argument used the NPC's primary or secondary Resonant Style (npc_behavior_v30.md §3.2 Belief Revision)
verdict.reasoning:
The critique's central claim — "there is no resolution mode where a party is legitimately convinced and updates a Belief as a non-damaging outcome" and "the only contest path to changed conviction is the Conviction Scar" — is refuted by the existing corpus. The contest ALREADY has a merits-based Belief-revision outcome:

1. BELIEF REVISION IS A DISTINCT CONTEST OUTCOME. designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §3.2 "Belief Revision": "An NPC revises a Belief when ALL of the following hold: 1. A Contest produces decisive outcome... against the NPC 2. The winning argument used the NPC's primary or secondary Resonant Style." This is exactly "persuaded on the merits" — and §6.2/§6.3 require the targeting argument to "specifically address a known Belief of the NPC" using Evidence/Consequence/Solidarity, i.e. on the merits. ED-390 names the explicit gate: "Belief revision triple gate (decisive + Resonant Style + Belief engagement)" (npc_behavior_v30 §10 open-items, line 1061).

2. THE SCAR AND THE REVISION ARE THE SAME EVENT, NOT MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE. The critique frames the Scar as the only path and the revision as absent. But the contest audit (designs/audit/2026-04-28-political-dynamics-session/15_stress_tests_batch3.md:388) states plainly: "Contest succeeds: Scar applied. Old Belief replaced by revised position." The decisive RS-targeted win simultaneously (a) accrues a Scar on the Conviction and (b) replaces the targeted Belief with the argued position. The §6.2 "they cannot dismiss" narration the critique quotes is the player-facing line for THIS merits-based revision, not a separate coercion mode. Conviction Scars from coercion/witnessing are a different source entirely — conviction_track_v1 §3 ties Scar accrual to WITNESSING Thread/ethical events, not to contest argument; contest argument is the §3.2 revision channel.

3. THE SUBSTRATE PRIMITIVES ARE ALREADY WIRED — except one tracked emission stub. The critique says belief_revised and the Sincerity Gate exist "everywhere EXCEPT the system whose entire job is persuasion." But §6.3's Solidarity row already references "the NPC's next Belief revision check" as a live contest mechanic, and key_type_registry_v30.md §state.belief_revised has an explicit STUB at line 899: "emitting_systems=[fieldwork] may under-attribute — npc_behavior §3 belief revision may also emit this; verify; tracked workplan #32 / ED-937." So the engine team has ALREADY identified that the contest's §3.2 revision should emit the Key and tracked it as ED-937 / workplan #32. The cut-scene routing (articulation §3.1 trigger #10) is therefore already designed to fire on the contest's revision once the emission attribution is closed.

What is genuinely true: the contest does NOT currently apply the Sincerity Gate to filter instrumental wins from genuine conversions, and the belief_revised Key emission from §3.2 is an open attribution item. But these are narrow refinements to an EXISTING merits-based resolution mode, not the construction of a missing one. The leverage is therefore low, not high: the "you changed my mind" churn beat is already producible (decisive + RS + Belief engagement → Belief replaced), and the design discipline correction is "close ED-937 emission + optionally gate sincerity," not "add an Inquiry resolution branch."
verdict.refined_improvement: Do NOT add a new "Inquiry resolution branch" or Revealing-only sincerity sub-mode — that would duplicate npc_behavior_v30 §3.2 Belief Revision, which already converts the loser on the merits (decisive win + correct Resonant Style + Belief engagement → "Old Belief replaced by revised position", per stress-test batch3 line 388). The actual, much smaller work items: (1) Close the already-tracked ED-937 / workplan #32: add npc_behavior (or contest) to emitting_systems for state.belief_revised in key_type_registry_v30.md §879 so the §3.2 revision emits the Key and routes to articulation §3.1 trigger #10 (the cut-scene path the critique wants is then live). (2) If a sincerity guard is desired, do not invent a new gate — reuse the existing Sincerity Gate (Spirit TN7 Ob1) already integrated in investigation_systems §306, applying it only to suppress the belief_revised emission when the winning argument is tagged [INSTRUMENTAL] (Obscuring styles), while leaving the Scar/Track outcome untouched. Both are post-resolution Key-emission refinements keyed off styles already tracked — no new resolver, consistent with the two-primitive engine discipline.

gaps.title: The recent distillation audit canonizes the bidirectional track as 'load-bearing, do not cut' — ratifying the four-into-one flattening at the engine level
gaps.research_basis: Part 0/D.4: the four senses are NON-COEXTENSIVE; collapsing them loses the questions that live in the gaps. Eristic (pure contest) is the degenerate type, so canonizing the contest AS the pure-contest kernel entrenches the degenerate mode as the engine's identity.
gaps.system_state: The 2026-06-28 distillation report identifies the social contest as an instance of the single 'sigma resolver' (will+advantage -> outcome) and lists the bidirectional persuasion track among primitives to preserve uncut.

locations.quote: The **sigma resolver** turns *will + advantage* into an *outcome* (the contest engine).
the **bidirectional persuasion track**; the unified Thread pool formula; the **Appraise-first contest**

gaps.critique: The distillation framing 'the contest IS the sigma kernel (will+advantage -> outcome)' is true AS FAR AS THE AGONISTIC GAME GOES, but it silently equates 'deliberation' with 'who has more will+advantage.' That is precisely the eristic reduction pragma-dialectics names degenerate. By marking the bidirectional track load-bearing and uncuttable, the audit risks freezing the flattening into the canonical engine vocabulary — making FG-1/FG-3 look like scope creep rather than the restoration of three legitimate games the procedure should support.
gaps.churn_relevance: Engine discipline says reuse the two primitives — and that is right. The fix is NOT a parallel subsystem; it is recognizing that the OTHER engine primitive (the armature dot-product, event->meaning-for-an-observer) is the natural home for the merit/inquiry/bargain dimensions. The sigma resolver decides WHO PRESSED HARDER; the dot-product decides WHAT IT MEANT — and bargain/truth/unity are meaning-questions. Routing them through the dot-product keeps discipline while undoing the flattening.
gaps.improvement: Reframe the contest in the canonical vocabulary as sigma-resolver (the per-exchange push) PLUS a post-contest armature-dot-product pass over the exchange ledger that classifies the outcome into the four senses: winner (track decisive + eristic), bargain (Compromise + multi-axis trade per FG-1), truth (Revealing-merit Decisive + belief_revised per FG-3), unity (Total Victory + Disposition convergence). Each emits a distinct Key. This adds NO resolver — it adds one dot-product read and four Key types, exactly the 'thin wrapper consumes Keys / applies a primitive / emits Keys' shape the report itself prescribes.

verdict.evidence_quote: At HEAD the two engines correctly DIVERGE at the resolution atom: combat resolves a single continuous sigma-leverage net to one winner per beat (core.resolve), while social_contest_v30 resolves discrete net-success margins with genuinely non-zero-sum outcomes (CROSS = no comparison/no strain, Compromise zone, productive stall).
verdict.reasoning:
The two cited quotes are verbatim accurate (distillation_coherence_report.md L41 "sigma resolver turns will+advantage into outcome (the contest engine)" and L152 "bidirectional persuasion track ... DO NOT cut"). And the four-senses / eristic-is-degenerate research basis is real — it lives in the sibling 2026-06-28 combat-critique corpus (recovered_reconcile_and_adversarial.md L327, L340: the "master equivocation" that combat is sense-3 zero-sum and deliberation is frequently sense-4 mixed-motive). So FG-5 is not fabricated.

But the central inference — that canonizing the contest as the sigma kernel "silently equates deliberation with who has more will+advantage" and entrenches eristic flattening — is OVERSTATED, on two independent grounds both grounded in the corpus:

(1) The distillation report does NOT flatten the four senses. Calling something "sigma Instance A" labels the per-exchange PUSH only; the report simultaneously marks load-bearing-uncuttable the very mechanics that carry the non-eristic outcomes: the "bidirectional persuasion track," the "Appraise-first contest," and (L194) the do-not-touch list. The spec those refer to (social_contest_v30.md §4 Step 4) already resolves to genuinely non-zero-sum outcomes that are not a single winner: CROSS (different genres — "No strain dealt. Neither argument attacked the other"), the Compromise zone (Track 4-6, §6), the productive stall (§2 Step 4 — "the failure to agree IS a consequential outcome"), and Chain/Deadlock Compromise (§6.3). The flattening FG-5 fears would require collapsing these to one continuous net + one winner — which the report explicitly preserves, not erases.

(2) The corpus's OWN adversarial verdict on this exact question reaches the opposite engine conclusion. recovered_reconcile_and_adversarial.md L327 explicitly REJECTS "forcing combat's continuous sigma-leverage μ-shift onto the social engine for 'one resolver to rule both' ... a single resolver that imposed a single continuous net + single winner would destroy CROSS/Compromise/stall." L340: "The corpus does NOT argue for a shared RESOLVER ... it argues for the opposite, and HEAD already got that right ... the two engines correctly DIVERGE at the resolution atom." So the same research base FG-5 cites concludes HEAD already AVOIDS the flattening; FG-5 reads the distillation report's "sigma" label as ratifying a flattening that the report's load-bearing list and the adversarial verdict both contradict.

On the improvement: FG-5's proposed fix routes merit/bargain/truth/unity through "a post-contest armature-dot-product pass that classifies the outcome into four senses and emits four Key types." This strains engine discipline. The armature dot-product (key_substrate_v30 §8.2, L152-156) is a per-OBSERVER interpretation primitive: it returns ONE scalar per NPC (Concern salience / Disposition shift / Memory salience) for a given Key and a given armature. It is not an outcome-classifier and cannot natively partition an exchange ledger into winner/bargain/truth/unity. Repurposing it that way is itself a new operation, not "one dot-product read." Moreover the bargain/negotiation dimension specifically — the one genuinely non-Track regime — the corpus says should NOT be a dot-product or Track operation at all: social_contest_v30 §12 self-flags "Negotiation compromise resolution (ZOPA-style) — identified as structurally different from Persuasion Track, not designed," and recovered_reconcile L314-320/L327 says a ZOPA/Nash resolver is the rare justified NEW subsystem precisely because the regime differs. So FG-5's "no new resolver, just reuse the dot-product" framing is the weakest part.

Net: the underlying tension (eristic-as-degenerate, four senses non-coextensive) is real and worth a note, but (a) the distillation report does not entrench the flattening — it preserves the non-zero-sum mechanics, (b) the corpus's own verdict says HEAD already diverges correctly, and (c) the proposed dot-product-classifier fix mis-models the dot-product and contradicts the corpus's ruling that the bargain sense wants its own resolver. Leverage drops from high to low: the actionable residue is editorial (have the distillation report note that "sigma Instance A" names only the per-exchange push, and that CROSS/Compromise/stall + the deferred ZOPA mode are the engine's non-eristic outcomes), not an engine change.
verdict.refined_improvement: Do NOT add a post-contest dot-product outcome-classifier or four new Key types — that mis-uses the armature dot-product (a per-observer interpretation scalar, key_substrate §8.2, not an outcome partitioner) and contradicts the corpus ruling that the bargain/ZOPA sense wants its own resolver, not the Track or the combat kernel (recovered_reconcile L327; social_contest §12). Instead, make this purely editorial: in distillation_coherence_report.md §1/§2, annotate that "sigma Instance A" labels ONLY the per-exchange push, and that the contest's NON-eristic outcomes already exist and are independently load-bearing — CROSS (no-comparison/no-strain, §4 Step 4), the Compromise zone (§6), the productive stall (§2 Step 4), and the DEFERRED ZOPA/negotiation resolver (§12, future work) for the genuine mixed-motive bargain. That records the four-senses distinction without freezing a flattening the engine does not actually have, and without bolting a subsystem onto the dot-product.

gaps.title: No in-contest commitment store: a contest is dice-margin attrition on a scalar bar, not an exchange of commitments
gaps.research_basis: Hamblin's commitment store (deliberation Part II.B-D): real argument is a move-and-commitment structure with a running ledger of conceded propositions that legal moves alter; Walton-Krabbe build six dialogue types on it; Dutilh Novaes shows even solitary deduction is an internalized Prover-Skeptic exchange. Macro-structure EMERGES from this granular ledger - it is the bottom-up substrate.
gaps.system_state: Across exchanges the contest persists only scalar attrition state - the Persuasion Track, a single transient Doubt Marker, Concentration, and strain. Argument content is consumed for dice bonuses and discarded; nothing a side asserts or concedes is recorded or constrains its later moves.

locations.quote: Range: 0–10. Side A wins ≥ 7. Side B wins ≤ 3. Compromise zone: 4–6.

Compare successes. Higher wins. Margin = difference.
- If margin > resistance → Persuasion Track moves (margin − resistance) toward winner's position.
| Composure | Charisma × 3 | 3–21 | Vitality = Endurance × 10 (ED-694) |

gaps.critique: The entire resolution loop is a hit-point model with the labels swapped: Composure parallels Vitality, strain parallels damage, Focus defence parallels Armour, and the Persuasion Track is the bar you push. The thing the research says is THE granular substrate - a ledger of what each party has conceded/committed, that makes later contradictory moves illegal or costly - does not exist. A side can argue Precedent in exchange 1 and Suppression of the same fact in exchange 3 with zero mechanical friction, because no exchange leaves a propositional trace. Winning is accumulated dice margin, not having built a winning position over a commitment structure (Hamblin/Lorenzen's definition of validity as a winning strategy over exactly this store).
gaps.churn_relevance: This is the project's own bottom-up-emergent axiom inverted. The macro outcome (who wins, who governs) is supposed to EMERGE from granular per-move substrate validated top-down; here it emerges from a scalar that integrates dice luck. The richest churn the research promises - a contradiction trap (you conceded X, now Y impeaches you), a forced retraction, an opponent pinned by their own prior commitment - is structurally impossible because there is nothing to be pinned to. The failure modes that would seed narrative (someone caught contradicting their record) cannot occur.
gaps.improvement: Add a Commitment Store as a per-side bucket the sigma resolver reads, reusing existing primitives - no new subsystem. Each exchange's declared style/claim writes one entry: {proposition, genre, orientation, source-if-cited}. Two engine hooks, both via existing math: (1) CONTRADICTION - if a side argues an orientation opposing its own prior committed entry on the same proposition, the sigma resolver applies an Ob bump or -Xd to that side (impeached by your own record), and the opponent who cites the contradiction gets the existing Recall +2D treatment. (2) CONCESSION - 'Concede a Point' (§4 Step 5) writes the opponent's last proposition into your store as conceded; a conceded proposition is then unavailable to you as an argument and gives the opponent a standing +1D whenever they invoke it. The store is a clock-class ledger (the distillation report already classes persuasion_track as a bidirectional clock and lists clocks like Evidence Track as canonical state); commitment entries are append-only clock ticks the resolver consumes.

verdict.evidence_quote: [social_contest_v30.md §8 Derived Values, line 423] "| Composure | Charisma × 3 | 3–21 | Vitality = Endurance × 10 (ED-694) |" — and the same table's Focus-defence row (line 425): "| Focus defence | floor(Foc ÷ 2) × 3 | 0–9 | Armour Rating (damage reduction), scaled ×3 |". The doc itself declares the HP/armour parallel the critique names.
verdict.reasoning: The gap is real and the cited quotes are all verbatim-accurate. The contest's only persisted cross-exchange state is scalar attrition: the Persuasion Track (params/contest.md line 100, "Range: 0–10"), strain toward Composure, Concentration, and a single transient Doubt Marker. sim/personal/contest.py confirms this at the implementation level — resolve_exchange and ExchangeResult/ContestResult carry only net successes, margin, track movement, and momentum; there is no propositional record, and build_argue_pool's `position` arg is documented as unused. I grepped designs/ for commitment|contradict|impeach|retract|concede|prior-statement|on-record|inconsistent: NO mechanic records a side's prior in-contest assertions or penalizes contradicting them. All "contradict" hits are Conviction-vs-action friction (e.g., arc_expansion ethical-framework +1 Ob) or world-state inconsistency — a different axis. The nearest candidates fall short: (1) "Concede a Point" (social_contest §4 Step 5) only grants +1D next exchange and records nothing about WHAT was conceded; (2) Resonant Style "Sanction" (contest_extensions.md line 47) invokes EXTERNAL "binding authority contradicting NPC's position," not the side's own prior moves; (3) Recall +2D (params/contest.md line 24) rewards citing an external verifiable claim, not impeaching an opponent's earlier exchange. So the research-named substrate — a per-side ledger of conceded/committed propositions that makes later contradictory moves costly — genuinely does not exist; the resolution loop is the attrition model the doc's own §8 "Parallel" column advertises. Leverage corrected high→medium: the absence removes one specific churn class (contradiction traps / forced retractions), but the contest is NOT churn-barren — Obligations (§6.1), Conviction Scars (§6.2), Chain Contests (§6.3), and Resonant Style targeting already generate substantial downstream churn. The "project's axiom inverted" framing is rhetorical overreach; the macro outcome does emerge from per-exchange granular rolls, just not from a propositional ledger.
verdict.refined_improvement: Add a per-side append-only Commitment Store as a clock-class ledger the sigma resolver reads — no new subsystem, consistent with the distillation report's clock taxonomy (it classes persuasion_track as a bidirectional clock and Evidence Track / conviction_scars as canonical clocks). Each exchange's declared style writes one entry {proposition, genre, orientation, source-if-cited}. Two hooks via existing math: (1) CONTRADICTION — if a side argues an orientation opposing its own prior committed entry on the same proposition, the resolver applies −Xd to that side's Argue pool (channel-correct per PP-716: actor-state/self-impeachment degradation routes to Pool, NOT Ob — the finding's "Ob bump OR −Xd" should be pinned to −Xd to honor the channel reservation that already governs Rattled/Spent/wounds), and the opponent citing it gets the existing Recall +2D treatment. (2) CONCESSION — "Concede a Point" (§4 Step 5) additionally writes the opponent's last proposition into your store as conceded; a conceded proposition becomes unavailable to you and grants the opponent a standing +1D when invoked. Scope/cost note: cap store reads to the contest scene (clears at scene end like strain/Concentration per §6) and gate CONTRADICTION on same-proposition matching to keep GM tracking tractable (cf. the §6.1 Obligation 3-item GM-advisory cap). This is squarely an Instance-A sigma consumer, addressing the report's own Rec #4 "verify social_contest on the sigma kernel" rather than expanding the resolver enum.

gaps.title: 'Concede a Point' is a hit-point bribe, not a recorded concession
gaps.research_basis: In commitment-store dialogue (Walton-Krabbe), 'concede' is the canonical move that ALTERS the store - it adds a proposition to your commitments that the other party may thereafter use freely and that you may not later deny. Concession is the load-bearing move that makes the structure bottom-up.
gaps.system_state: The contest's 'Concede a Point' forfeits the exchange for a mechanical kicker and records nothing about what was conceded.

locations.quote: **Concede a Point:** Forfeit exchange. Take 1 strain. Persuasion Track moves +1 toward non-forfeiting side. Gain +1D on next exchange.
| Concede a Point | 1 | +1 toward non-forfeiting side | +1D next exchange |

gaps.critique: The system uses the exact word from the research - 'Concede' - for a move that is its opposite: a tempo trade (eat 1 strain + 1 track now, buy +1D next exchange) with no propositional content. Nothing is conceded in the dialogical sense; nothing enters any store; the +1D rebound actually makes conceding a tactical attack rather than a commitment. This is the single sharpest place where the existing mechanic NAMES the right primitive and then does none of its work.
gaps.churn_relevance: A genuine concession is high-value churn: it hands the opponent a proposition they can weaponize for the rest of the contest and that constrains the conceder's future framing - exactly the kind of consequential decision the churn axiom wants. The current version generates no narrative residue; the conceded point evaporates at exchange end.
gaps.improvement: Re-spec 'Concede a Point' to write the opponent's active proposition into BOTH stores as a conceded entry (reusing the FG-1 ledger). Keep the +1 track/strain cost, but replace the unconditional +1D-next-exchange with a CONDITIONAL bonus: the opponent gains +1D whenever they invoke the conceded point, and the conceder takes an Ob bump (or is barred) if they later argue against it. This turns a tempo trade into a real commitment using only existing dice-channel math (the -1D/+Ob channels already reserved per PP-716).

verdict.evidence_quote: **Concede a Point:** Forfeit exchange. Take 1 strain. Persuasion Track moves +1 toward non-forfeiting side. Gain +1D on next exchange.
verdict.reasoning: Both cited quotes are verbatim-accurate (social_contest_v30.md §4 Step 5 line 187; params/contest.md "Forfeit Actions" table line 128). The factual core of the critique holds: "Concede a Point" is purely a tempo/strain trade and records nothing propositional. I grepped the entire corpus for commitment-store / conceded-entry / proposition / standing-claim semantics: nothing exists in the personal-scale social contest. The only "concession" mechanic that records content is the faction-layer TREATY system (faction_layer_v30.md §3.3 "Concession declaration" — "Players/GM declare demands and concessions"), which is a different module at a different scale and does not feed back into a contest's exchange resolution. contest_extensions.md, npc_behavior, conviction_taxonomy, and knots contain no proposition-ledger either. The sim (sim/personal/contest.py) does not model any conceded content; the worked example tests/sim/sim_thread_01.md treats Concede a Point as exactly the tempo move the critique describes ("forfeit exchange, take 1 strain, track +1... gain +1D next exchange"). So the gap is real and NOT already-handled anywhere. The naming irony is also real — "Concede" is the canonical commitment-store move and the system uses the word for its functional opposite (the +1D rebound makes conceding an attack). Leverage corrected from high to MEDIUM: the improvement is entirely parasitic on FG-1 — it explicitly says "reusing the FG-1 ledger" / "write... into BOTH stores." No store/ledger currently exists in the corpus (grep for FG-1/ledger/proposition in contest_extensions returned nothing), so FG-2 cannot land independently; it is a rider on a larger FG-1 subsystem. That coupling, plus the fact that the current mechanic is a working (if mis-named) tempo lever rather than a broken or inert one, caps real leverage below "high." It is a genuine churn-and-fiction improvement, not a correctness fix.
verdict.refined_improvement: Adopt only if FG-1's commitment store is also adopted (FG-2 has no standalone home — there is currently no store/ledger object in the contest corpus to write into). With FG-1 present: on Concede a Point, write the opponent's active proposition (the style/genre+orientation claim they just argued) into the shared store as a conceded entry keyed to the conceder. Keep the +1 track and 1 strain. Replace the unconditional +1D-next-exchange with a CONDITIONAL channel-discipline form that respects PP-716 (actor-state and relational effects route to Pool/Track, never +Ob unless it is a genuine difficulty source): (a) when the opponent later invokes the conceded point, they gain +1D on that exchange's Argue (the conceded proposition is now free ammunition); (b) if the conceder later argues AGAINST the conceded point, apply -1D to that Argue (self-contradiction penalty via the reserved Pool channel) rather than an Ob bump, so it stays consistent with how Rattled/Spent/wounds are modeled. This keeps all math on existing dice-channel rails and adds no new resolver — it is bookkeeping on the sigma-A contest kernel plus one store write, not a bolt-on subsystem. If FG-1 is NOT adopted, the minimal standalone version is purely fictional: rename the move (e.g. "Yield Ground") so the system stops claiming the Walton-Krabbe "concede" primitive it does not implement — a zero-cost honesty fix that removes the naming irony without new mechanics.

gaps.title: Doubt Marker is the embryo of a commitment store but is collapsed to a single transient -2
gaps.research_basis: The commitment store is a multi-entry, persistent ledger of challenges and concessions; a challenge stays open on the record until answered. The research's richness is precisely that many propositions are simultaneously in play and constrain each other.
gaps.system_state: Obscuring wins place a Doubt Marker that reduces the opponent's NEXT winning margin by 2; only one is active at a time, new replaces old, consumed on use.

locations.quote:
Only one Doubt Marker active at a time. New replaces old.
- Consumed on use.
Doubt Marker effect: opponent's next winning exchange has its margin reduced by 2 before resistance is applied (minimum 0).

gaps.critique: The Doubt Marker is the one in-contest mechanic that gestures at persistence - it is a mark left ON THE OPPONENT by an argument move. But it is collapsed to a singleton scalar penalty (-2, one active, consumed) rather than a ledger of open doubts attached to specific propositions. The design space the research opens - multiple unanswered challenges accumulating against named claims, each answerable by a justify move - has been compressed to a single throwaway debuff. This is under-configuration: the primitive exists in miniature and was not generalized.
gaps.churn_relevance: Generalizing Doubt into a store of open challenges-against-claims is directly the churn axiom: an unanswered doubt is a story hook (the claim everyone remembers was never rebutted). A single consumed -2 generates no residue.
gaps.improvement: Promote the Doubt Marker from a singleton to an entry-type in the FG-1 commitment store: an Obscuring win files a 'doubt' entry against the opponent's specific committed proposition. Doubt entries persist (clock-class, not consumed) and each imposes the existing -2 to margin when the doubted proposition is re-argued, until the opponent spends an exchange to 'justify' (a Recall/Argue roll that clears it). This reuses the existing -2 magnitude and the existing resolution roll - it just attaches them to propositions and lets more than one exist.

verdict.reasoning:
The factual core is accurate. Cited quotes are verbatim: designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md §4 lines 180-183 ("Place a Doubt Marker on the opponent. / Doubt Marker effect: opponent's next winning exchange has its margin reduced by 2... / Only one Doubt Marker active at a time. New replaces old. / Consumed on use.") and the mirror in params/contest.md line 95. The Doubt Marker IS a singleton scalar, and no per-proposition claim/doubt ledger exists anywhere in the corpus (grep for "commitment store"/"claim ledger"/FG-1 across designs/ returns nothing; sim/personal/contest.py does not even implement the Doubt Marker). So the under-configuration as a local code observation is real.

But leverage is overstated on two grounds. (1) The persistence/churn function the critique wants the contest does already produce — just not bound to named propositions. The corpus carries durable contest residue via Obligations ("a mechanical commitment that persists across seasons... the contest's lasting consequence in the game world", social_contest_v30.md §7 line 242), Conviction Scars that "persist... do not heal between chain contests" (line 304), Suspicion Tokens (contest_extensions Sanction row line 47), permanent Read knowledge on stall (line 76), plus Knot/Disposition movement. The Doubt Marker is deliberately the EPHEMERAL in-contest tactical layer; the story-hook residue lives in those primitives. "A single consumed -2 generates no residue" is true of that marker but false of the contest's outcome model overall — the churn the axiom wants is mostly already produced. (2) The improvement is contingent on the non-existent "FG-1 commitment store" and would introduce a per-proposition entity ledger (named claims tracked as objects, doubt entries filed against them, a new "justify" clearing roll). The contest engine does not currently model propositions as entities; this is a bolt-on subsystem, not pure reuse of the sigma resolver / armature dot-product. Real but medium→low leverage and bounded scope.
verdict.evidence_quote: A Decisive win (Persuasion Track ≥ 7 or ≤ 3) in a Formal or Grand Contest produces a binding **Obligation** — a mechanical commitment that persists across seasons. The Obligation is the contest's lasting consequence in the game world, not just a stat change.
verdict.refined_improvement: Do NOT introduce a new per-proposition entity ledger or a "justify" resolution roll, and do not make this contingent on the unbuilt FG-1 store. If any change is made, the minimal engine-disciplined version is: (a) allow more than one Doubt Marker to be active at once (drop the singleton/replace rule) so multiple obscuring wins each leave a -2, reusing the existing magnitude and the existing per-exchange margin computation — no new roll, no proposition objects; and (b) on a Decisive contest outcome, surface any unconsumed Doubt Marker as narrative residue routed through the EXISTING durable channels (a Suspicion Token or a -1 Disposition/Knot nudge on the doubted party) rather than vanishing. This produces the desired story-hook ("the doubt was never answered") using primitives already on the bus, without modeling named claims as entities. Even this is optional polish, not a structural gap.

gaps.title: Succession is hard-coded as pure agon, where history/theory show it is the canonical site of alea and mimicry
gaps.research_basis: Caillois: succession across pre-1600 polities was alea (primogeniture/lot — deliberately removing the prize to KILL FACTION), agon (tanistry/kurultai), OR mimicry (acclamation). The Venetian doge was an engineered ALEA+AGON hybrid specifically to defeat capture. Routing all succession through a single merit contest is precisely the configuration those polities engineered AGAINST, because pure-agon succession maximizes faction capture and post-contest grievance.
gaps.system_state: §7.2 routes every faction's leadership transition through Grand Contest infrastructure (§3–§5) — i.e. the sigma agon kernel — with adjudicator and outcome tables but no lot and no acclamation path.

locations.quote: The Succession Contest uses Grand Contest infrastructure (§3-§5) with the following adaptations:
Decisive (≥ 7 or ≤ 3) | Single winner takes faction leadership. Other claimants face Disposition penalty with the new leader
Without her, the succession is purely institutional. Parliament selects.

gaps.critique: The corpus's own prose says Hafenmark succession is 'purely institutional. Parliament selects' and that Crown succession runs on 'deed-presumption' / bloodline (npc_character_analyses), but the MECHANISM in §7.2 collapses all of that into agon. The pure-agon configuration also fights the philosophy directly: it hard-codes the macro outcome (who governs) as the winner of one dice contest, exactly the 'hard-coded macro outcome is a smell' the design standard warns against — except here the smell is the opposite mistake, forcing an emergent-looking contest onto institutions whose real legitimacy logic is bloodline (alea) or acclamation (mimicry). It also suppresses the anti-capture churn: a lot-element makes a powerful claimant's victory uncertain, which is the engine that historically generated the antechamber-maneuver / coalition-buying drama.
gaps.churn_relevance: Pure-agon succession produces ONE churn shape (winner+resentful losers). Alea succession produces a different, richer shape: the favorite who can be denied by the draw, the desperate pre-draw coalition-buying, the legitimacy crisis of a lot-chosen leader. Collapsing four families to one collapses four distinct churn generators to one.
gaps.improvement: Add a succession-mode selector keyed to the faction's legitimacy type (already enumerated in §7.2's adjudicator list and in precedents_warfare §5 Weber types). (a) ALEA-HYBRID (Crown bloodline / Venetian doge): claimants run a SHORTENED Grand Contest to qualify; the final winner is drawn by weighted lot among the top-2 finishers, weight = Mandate-leverage already computed by the sigma kernel. Reuse contest.py's existing `random.Random()` (currently only an initiative tiebreak) as the resolution draw. The faction-killing property emerges for free: a strong claimant cannot guarantee the throne, so factions must hedge -> churn. (b) Keep pure agon as the explicit mode for merit-institutions (Löwenritter seniority->contest). The selector is a single field; the buckets (track, Mandate, lot) already exist.

verdict.evidence_quote: Jarl Assembly elects the player as Vaynard's Regent ... OR as designated successor ... succession-eligible (Varfell elective succession per SUC-01)
verdict.reasoning:
All three cited quotes verify verbatim (social_contest_v30.md §7.2 line 328; resolution table line 340; npc_character_analyses_v30.md line 241 "the succession is purely institutional. Parliament selects"; plus the deed-presumption quote at line 156). So the locations are real. But the critique's framing — that the corpus collapses ALL succession to a single agon kernel and provides "no acclamation path" — is overstated, because two of its three Caillois families already have homes.

MIMICRY/acclamation is NOT unfilled. designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md gives Varfell an explicit elective/acclamation succession: Standing-7 is reached when the "Jarl Assembly elects" or Vaynard names a successor "at a public Assembly," with the core note that "The Jarl Assembly's role is analogous to the Varangian Guard's role in Byzantine acclamation politics — the Assembly creates the legitimacy it affirms." Separately, the contest-groundup audit (designs/audit/2026-06-03-contest-groundup/modes.py, the engine's pluggable-win-condition rework) already implements TallyAtClose / VoteAtClose (assembly vote), public_oration (TallyAtClose "who wins the room"), GraceThreshold (sovereign grants/denies), and a ceremonial mode whose "win = acclamation." The groundup audit's framing memo states outright that "pluggable win-conditions are required." So acclamation is mechanically present, not absent.

BLOODLINE/designation is NOT pure agon either. Crown Standing-7 succession fires when "named by Almud as the succession candidate OR has built a 3-of-5 inner-circle supermajority" — designation, not a dice contest. Torben's claim is "Mandate + Bloodline Legitimacy bonus (+2D, Bloodline claim tier per LIN-01)" (baralta_crown_claim_v30 §4). Hafenmark is "Parliament selects." §7.2 itself gates eligibility on "a structural claim mechanism (Deed-Claim, Bloodline, institutional precedent)" and assigns a per-faction adjudicator (Crown inner circle by Disposition; Hafenmark Parliament by Mandate-weighted vote; Varfell Jarl Assembly by quorum; etc.). So the legitimacy logic is already differentiated; what is single is the RESOLUTION (the §7.2 outcome table reads off one Persuasion Track regardless of family).

The one genuinely sound, non-overstated residue is ALEA. There is no sortition / weighted-lot / random-draw win-condition anywhere — not in the live spec, not in sim/personal/contest.py, and notably not even among the groundup prototype's six cross-cultural venues, which modeled the Venetian SECRET COUNCIL but not the doge's lot mechanism. The finding's distinctive churn argument (a favourite who can be denied by the draw -> pre-draw coalition-buying -> the faction-killing anti-capture property) is real and unaddressed. That is the finding's actual contribution, and it is narrower than "succession is mis-configured as pure agon."

Two accuracy issues lower the leverage from high to medium: (1) the win-condition collapse the finding decries is already mid-repair in the corpus (the groundup pluggable-win-condition rework, dated 2026-06-03/04), though that prototype is NOT yet wired into the live social_contest_v30.md or contest.py — so the live spec genuinely is agon-resolution-only, but the fix is in flight, not unconceived; (2) the improvement misstates the engine: it says contest.py's random.Random() is "currently only an initiative tiebreak," but at sim/personal/contest.py line 145-151 the rng is the MAIN resolution-roll RNG feeding roll_pool for both sides, not a tiebreak.
verdict.refined_improvement: Narrow the finding to its true residue: add an ALEA win-condition, and route it through the already-in-flight pluggable-win-condition work rather than a parallel selector. (1) Do not "add a succession-mode selector" as a new subsystem — the groundup audit (designs/audit/2026-06-03-contest-groundup/modes.py/resolver.py) already establishes win-conditions as the pluggable axis (ProofBar, ThresholdRace, TallyAtClose, VoteAtClose, GraceThreshold). The missing member is a Lot/WeightedLot win-condition; add it there (e.g. a WeightedDrawAtClose that takes the top-k contestants' accumulated standing/Mandate as draw weights), so it composes with the existing resolver instead of bolting onto §7.2. (2) When that audit is integrated into the live spec, §7.2's per-faction adjudicator list (which already enumerates the legitimacy bodies) becomes the natural place to bind each faction's succession win-condition: Crown/doge-type -> shortened qualifying contest then WeightedDrawAtClose among top-2 (the anti-capture property the finding wants); Varfell -> the elective/acclamation path that faction_politics already specifies (TallyAtClose / Jarl Assembly), so DO NOT re-author Varfell — it is done; Löwenritter/merit -> keep ThresholdRace agon. (3) Drop the claim about reusing random.Random() as "an initiative tiebreak" — it is already the resolution-roll RNG; the new draw should be a distinct, auditable weighted-draw call, not a repurposing of the existing roll, to keep anti-fabrication/sim discipline clean. Net: this is a medium-leverage win-condition gap (alea), not a high-leverage "succession is mis-configured as pure agon" gap.

gaps.title: No mimicry/acclamation resolution mode — even where the corpus already names 'consensus' and 'acclamation'-shaped selection
gaps.research_basis: Caillois MIMICRY in politics = performed legitimacy: acclamation, quasi-inspiration, sovereignty-as-performance. Canonical Church election had THREE modes — scrutiny (agon), compromise (delegated agon), and quasi-inspiration (mimicry, a spontaneous unanimous acclamation treated as the Spirit speaking). A deliberative game that omits mimicry cannot represent the legitimacy-by-performance that drives sacral and charismatic polities.
gaps.system_state: Every resolution in the spec terminates in a Persuasion-Track number or exchange-majority count (agon). 'Consensus' governance for Warden/RM/Guilds is described narratively but has no contest-side resolution procedure; the 'Crowd' adjudicator is just a Charisma-keyed pool, not an acclamation mode.

locations.quote: Crowd | A collective audience reacts to delivery and force | Parliamentary session, public forum, street crowd | Charisma
Warden governance uses consensus model with collective Spirit-derived governance stat.
node-based meritocratic consensus cells — governance through equity, participation, care, and collaborative decision-making

gaps.critique: The 'Crowd' adjudicator looks like it might be ilinx/mimicry, but mechanically it only swaps the primary attribute to Charisma inside the SAME agon kernel — the crowd is a dice-pool modifier, not a distinct resolution family. So a parliamentary acclamation and a tribunal verdict resolve by identical math. Meanwhile the corpus explicitly posits consensus/acclamation institutions (Warden 'consensus model', RM 'collaborative decision-making', Church College of Cardinals) and then has no procedure for them to reach a binding outcome EXCEPT by routing through agon. This is under-configuration, not pure absence — the seam exists, the family does not. The Church especially is mis-served: its real-world prototype had quasi-inspiration as a first-class mode, and Valoria's Church is the most sacral faction, yet its tribunals are configured as the most rigid asymmetric agon (Persuasion Track pre-biased to 6–7).
gaps.churn_relevance: Mimicry generates churn agon cannot: a leader chosen by acclamation has legitimacy that is brittle and performative — it can collapse if the performance fails, producing the 'the acclamation was hollow' arc. Agon-only succession can never produce a legitimacy that is real-but-unearned, which is the engine of pretender and usurper stories.
gaps.improvement: Add an ACCLAMATION resolution mode reusing the armature dot-product (the existing event->meaning-for-an-observer primitive). Instead of accumulating margin on a Persuasion Track, the proposal is evaluated against each present adjudicator-NPC's Conviction armature; if a supermajority (e.g. >=80% of weighted Mandate/Disposition) returns positive significance in a SINGLE evaluation, it passes by acclamation — no exchanges. Crucially, a failed acclamation does not just stall: it converts to standard agon with a starting Track penalty (the failed performance damaged the proposer), generating the hollow-acclamation churn. This is a new resolution branch, not a new subsystem: it consumes the same Conviction matrix the NPC engine already runs and emits the same Obligation Keys.

verdict.evidence_quote: Ceremonial (praise / blame). An audience as spectator in the present; nothing is *at issue* — the opposite of a contested case. It builds or spends standing rather than resolving a dispute. Win = a standing shift / acclamation.
verdict.reasoning: The gap is real and verified against the live canonical spec, not just inferred. social_contest_v30.md is marked "Status: CANONICAL — approved 2026-04-17". Its §2 Step 1 adjudicator table has exactly four types (Expert judge=Cognition, Crowd=Charisma, No adjudicator=Attunement, Panel=Cognition); all four feed the SAME Persuasion-Track / Argue-pool kernel and differ only by primary attribute (confirmed in params/contest.md: "Argue (Crowd) | (Charisma × 2) + History bonus", and the §2 reasoning table "Crowd | Charisma | Crowd responds to delivery and authority"). So the finding's core claim — the Crowd type is a dice-pool attribute swap inside the agon kernel, not a distinct resolution family — is literally true. The consensus/acclamation institutions are named but given no contest-side procedure: campaign_architecture_v30.md §6.3 makes Warden "Consensus governance (not hierarchical). Governance stat = average Spirit of active Menders" (a stat, not a resolution mode), and §7.2 Succession Contest routes EVERY consensus body through the Grand Contest Persuasion Track — "Church: College of Cardinals by Disposition; ... RM: Mandate ≥ 3 organizers by consensus" all resolve by the same Track outcome table (Decisive/Compromise/Total Victory). I grepped the whole personal contest pipeline: sim/personal/contest.py and params/contest_extensions.md contain NO match for acclamation/armature/win-condition/consensus — the resolution surface is agon-only in code as well as spec. The Church mis-service claim also checks out: §7 "Persuasion Track starts biased at 6" / Church Tribunal at 6-7 is the most rigid asymmetric agon, the opposite of quasi-inspiration. Decisively, this gap was independently surfaced by a prior internal audit (2026-06-03 contest-groundup), which I located: framework.md §B lists discourse modes that "do not share a win condition" and names "Ceremonial (praise / blame) ... Win = a standing shift / acclamation"; AUDIT_MODULATION.md states "The win condition is one shape ... Vote tallies ... consensus are not expressible. → make the win-condition pluggable"; HISTORICAL_VALIDATION.md likewise lists "ceremonial by acclamation" as a required pluggable win-condition. That audit is explicitly scaffolding ("proposals for Jordan's call, not decisions") and none of it reached the canonical spec — so the gap is genuinely open, and FG-3 is a faithful re-derivation of an already-recognized but unclosed seam. Leverage is correctly "medium": real churn payoff (hollow/brittle performative legitimacy, the pretender/usurper engine, aligned with the world-churn axiom), but it is an additive branch on a functioning system and the design groundwork already exists, so not "high".
verdict.refined_improvement: Keep the proposal's spirit (reuse the armature dot-product, emit Obligation Keys, failed acclamation converts to standard agon with a Track penalty) but tighten it to the engine's actual two-primitive vocabulary as named in the 2026-06-28 distillation report: the sigma resolver and the armature dot-product (`Σ axis: observer.armature[axis] × key.symbolic_dimensions[axis] × key.impact_vector[axis]`, distillation_coherence_report.md). Do NOT introduce a fifth adjudicator type or a bolt-on subsystem; instead add ACCLAMATION as a *pluggable win-condition* on the existing Crowd adjudicator — exactly the "make the win-condition pluggable" lever the 2026-06-03 audit already prescribed (AUDIT_MODULATION.md), which keeps it inside the canonical-spec frame rather than reopening §2's fixed-type rule. Mechanically: on contest open with a Crowd/collective adjudicator whose members carry Conviction armatures, attempt a single armature-dot-product pass over present adjudicator-NPCs weighted by Mandate/Disposition; if a supermajority returns positive significance, pass by acclamation in one evaluation (no exchanges, emit the same Obligation Key §6.1 a Decisive win would). On failure, fall through to the standard Persuasion Track with a starting penalty — reusing the existing §6 chain-contest mechanism of starting the Track at a non-5 position rather than inventing a new penalty unit. This consumes the Conviction matrix the NPC engine already runs (conviction_taxonomy_v30) and the same Disposition/Mandate weights Succession Contests already use, so it adds zero new state. Defer the lever magnitudes (the >=80% threshold, the Track penalty size) to sim-validation per the contest-groundup scope note — propose them as starting values, not canon.

gaps.title: No alea/sortition anywhere as an anti-capture device — the only randomness is intra-agon noise
gaps.research_basis: Caillois ALEA as a political technology: sortition removes the PRIZE from a contest, thereby killing faction (no point capturing a process that ends in a draw); the Venetian doge interleaved lot stages with election stages precisely to break capture chains. A deliberative game with zero lot mechanic cannot represent the entire historical class of anti-faction, anti-capture institutions.
gaps.system_state: The system contains no sortition resolution. Searching the contest pipeline, the only stochastic element is a tiebreak: contest.py instantiates `random.Random()` solely to break net-success ties in first-to-speak, and the registry notes 'Tie: random' for the same micro-decision.

locations.quote:
import random
        rng = random.Random()
Higher net = first-to-speak holder = declares last (information advantage). Tie: random. Transfers to exchange winner. Draws: holder retains.
Persuasion Track ≥ 7 = motion passes; ≤ 3 = motion fails; 4–6 = referred to committee.

gaps.critique: What randomness exists is NOISE INSIDE agon (which speaker goes second), not a resolution FAMILY (who wins). The d10 pool already embeds variance, but variance-in-a-merit-contest is still agon — the better pool still expects to win. True alea is when the OUTCOME is structurally severed from merit, and that is exactly what the corpus lacks. This is a real gap because Valoria has the perfect alea host: deadlocked parliamentary votes currently 'refer to committee' (an inert deferral) and Deadlocked chain contests resolve to a cold equilibrium freeze — both are precisely the situations where historical bodies reached for the lot to break capture. The system has the trigger condition and reaches for a non-result instead of the anti-faction tool the research prescribes.
gaps.churn_relevance: Sortition is the highest-leverage churn device in the research because its CHURN IS THE POINT: a lot-broken deadlock installs a leader nobody fully backs, immediately seeding legitimacy contests, buyer's-remorse coalitions, and 'the dice chose wrongly' arcs. Replacing 'referred to committee' (inert) with a lot is converting an inert mechanic into a churn generator — the exact move the axiom demands.
gaps.improvement: Add a SORTITION tiebreak as the canonical resolver for terminal deadlock, reusing the RNG already in contest.py. (a) BG Vote 'referred to committee' and chain-Deadlock 'cold equilibrium': instead of freezing, draw the outcome by lot weighted by each side's final Mandate/Track position (so it is alea-tinged-by-agon, the doge pattern, not flat coin-flip). (b) Optionally expose a 'Lot Charter' faction property (RM/Guild consensus cells) where contested internal offices resolve by pure lot among qualifying Standing>=5 members — the faction-killing institution the corpus's 'consensus cells' gesture at but never mechanize. Seed the draw per the Key-substrate's per-emission seeding rule (key_substrate_v30 'Every random draw is seeded per Key emission') so it is replayable.

verdict.evidence_quote: **Adjudicator:** the faction's institutional body (Crown: inner circle by majority Disposition; Hafenmark: Parliament by Mandate-weighted vote; Varfell: Jarl Assembly by quorum; Church: College of Cardinals by Disposition; Löwenritter: surviving Knight-Commanders by seniority; Guilds: Council by Favour-weighted vote; RM: Mandate ≥ 3 organizers by consensus).
verdict.reasoning: Every cited quote verifies verbatim. contest.py:147-148 instantiates `random.Random()` and uses it only for d10 pool rolls plus the first-to-speak tiebreak (line 165: tied exchange moves track +1 toward holder); canonical_registry.md:192 confirms "Tie: random"; social_contest_v30.md:509-510 confirms BG Vote "4–6 = referred to committee" with the zero-zero case also deferring to committee. I independently checked the system's natural alea hosts and confirmed the gap is unfilled: (1) The Succession Contest §7.2 (the textbook sortition use-case — contested office on leader removal) resolves entirely by agon: it "uses Grand Contest infrastructure (§3-§5)" and the adjudicator rule (quoted) lists majority Disposition / Mandate-weighted vote / quorum / seniority / Favour-weighted vote / consensus — no lot anywhere. Even RM, the explicitly anarchist faction whose lore gestures at lot-like governance ("node-based meritocratic consensus cells — governance through equity, participation, care", campaign_architecture_v30.md:55), resolves succession by "Mandate ≥ 3 organizers by consensus", not by lot — exactly the "gesture at but never mechanize" the finding describes. (2) The two terminal non-result sinks are real: chain Deadlock max-chain-3 produces a "cold equilibrium" that freezes Disposition for 4 seasons (social_contest_v30.md:311), and BG Vote 4-6 "referred to committee" has no downstream mechanics anywhere in the corpus. (3) A full-corpus grep for sortition/alea/by-lot/lottery returns zero resolution mechanics — the only "random draw" reference is the seeding-discipline rule at key_substrate_v30.md:393. So the critique's central claim — randomness is intra-agon noise, never a resolution family, and the perfect alea hosts (deadlock/committee) reach for a non-result instead — holds exactly as described. I corrected leverage from high to medium: the finding is real and the trigger conditions genuinely exist, but the two sinks are low-frequency terminal states (max-chain-3 after three Compromises; the narrow 4-6 BG band) rather than common outcomes, so the churn payoff, while real, fires rarely. The improvement is also engine-disciplined in its core (reuses the existing RNG and the per-Key-emission seeding rule that I confirmed exists), which keeps it from being a high-cost bolt-on.
verdict.refined_improvement: Core (disciplined, low-effort): make SORTITION the canonical resolver for the two existing terminal non-results, reusing the RNG already in contest.py and seeding per the key_substrate_v30.md:393 per-emission rule so draws are replayable. (a) Replace chain-Deadlock "cold equilibrium" (social_contest_v30.md:311) and BG Vote "4–6 referred to committee" (line 509) with a lot weighted by each side's final Mandate/Track position — alea-tinged-by-agon (the doge pattern), not a flat coin flip. This converts two inert freezes into churn: a lot-installed outcome nobody fully backs seeds legitimacy contests and "the dice chose wrongly" arcs. Crucially, route the lot outcome back through the existing Key bus as a normal emission so it propagates like any other contest result, rather than adding a parallel resolution path. (b) [Optional, higher cost — defer] A "Lot Charter" faction property for RM/Guild consensus cells where contested internal offices resolve by pure lot among Standing≥5 members is a genuinely new subsystem property and a faction-config addition; it should be scoped separately and only if §7.2 Succession is to support a non-agon adjudicator class, since today the corpus has no lot-based adjudicator at all.

gaps.title: Ilinx (crowd-charge / sacral fervor) is absent as a force — collapsed into Charisma-as-pool
gaps.research_basis: Caillois ILINX in politics = the crowd-roar, the charismatic/sacral charge that overwhelms deliberation rather than weighing it. The companion treats ilinx as a real resolution pressure (the acclamation that becomes a stampede, the charismatic surge that suspends procedure). It is distinct from mimicry (performed legitimacy) and from agon (weighed merit): ilinx is affect that BYPASSES the weighing.
gaps.system_state: Crowd affect enters only as the Charisma-keyed Argue pool and the Charisma modifier to strain; there is no mechanic where collective affect overrides the track or suspends the exchange structure.

locations.quote: Crowd | Charisma | Crowd responds to delivery and authority
Charisma modifier: max(0, floor((Charisma − 3) ÷ 2)) × 3
The galleries fill. The merchants send observers. By the end of the first week, every faction in Valoria knows a Grand Debate is coming.

gaps.critique: Charisma-as-pool is still agon — a higher-Charisma orator wins MORE RELIABLY, which is the opposite of ilinx, where the crowd's surge can carry a weak case past a strong one. The narrative corpus clearly WANTS ilinx (the gallery-filling Grand Debate, RM 'Popular Will' advancing 'when communities see organised dissatisfaction') but the contest engine has no channel for it. This is the mildest of the four gaps because it borders mimicry, but it is a distinct under-configuration: there is no mechanic by which accumulated crowd affect (a fervor clock) can spike and override the deliberative weighing.
gaps.churn_relevance: Ilinx churn is the mob/zealotry arc: a charismatic surge installs an outcome the careful agon would have rejected, then the surge dissipates and the actors must live with what the roar decided. Without ilinx the system cannot generate the 'the room got carried away' consequence class.
gaps.improvement: Reuse the existing clock bucket: add a FERVOR clock to Crowd-adjudicated contests that fills from Total-Victory exchanges, Belief-aligned wins, and Practitioner-Weaving spectacle. At a threshold, fervor triggers a one-shot override: the trailing side may force an immediate resolution at the current Track position regardless of remaining exchanges (the crowd will not wait). This bolts onto the existing Composure/Momentum economy and emits the same outcome Keys — no parallel system. Gate it to Crowd adjudicator only, preserving Expert-Judge/Panel as pure agon.

verdict.evidence_quote: | Crowd | Charisma | Crowd responds to delivery and authority |  ...  Charisma modifier: max(0, floor((Charisma − 3) ÷ 2)) × 3
verdict.reasoning: The system-state claim is accurate. I read the full social_contest_v30.md, params/contest.md, and contest_extensions.md. Crowd affect enters ONLY as the Charisma-keyed Argue pool (§3 table: Crowd → Charisma) and the Charisma modifier to strain (§4 CLASH/REINFORCE). Every other lever — Doubt Marker, Momentum (§9.5 Belief-aligned win = +1 Momentum = bonus successes), Composure, Total Victory, the per-exchange resistance erosion (ED-864) — rewards the higher-success orator, i.e. pure agon. The distillation_coherence_report confirms the architecture has exactly two primitives (sigma resolver + armature dot-product); the contest exchange is sigma-A (line 201). There is NO primitive in which accumulated collective affect spikes and overrides the deliberative weighing. I grepped the entire designs/ and repo for fervor|acclamation|stampede|mob|crowd surge|ilinx: the only 'fervour' hits are the mass-battle 'Crusade Fervour' tactic card (unrelated), and 'acclamation' appears only in (a) faction_politics §197 as flavor analogy and (b) the SIBLING ground-up audit (2026-06-03-contest-groundup/framework.md §B) which lists Ceremonial/acclamation as a DISTINCT win-condition — but that is an explicit un-adopted proposal ('proposals for Jordan's call, not decisions'), not canon. So the gap is genuinely unfilled in the active system. TWO CAVEATS that pull this toward 'sound but mild': (1) The finding's narrative-demand evidence is partly weak — it cites RM 'Popular Will' as proof the corpus WANTS ilinx, but Popular Will is STRUCK from canon (clock_registry_v30 line 30 marks it PROVISIONAL/STRUCK per ED-795; clock_registry_staleness_report line 29: 'Popular Will (PW) | STRUCK'; workplan ED-747 verifies the strike). Leaning on a deprecated track overstates the corpus's pull toward ilinx. The Grand Debate gallery quote in arcs_31_35 is real but is atmosphere, not a mechanic crying out for an override. (2) The finding itself rates leverage 'low' and calls it 'the mildest of the four gaps' — honest self-assessment; corrected_leverage stays low.
verdict.refined_improvement: Keep the FERVOR clock (clocks are a sanctioned non-rolling primitive per the two-primitive distillation, so this is engine-legal, not a parallel subsystem). Gate it to the Crowd adjudicator only, leaving Expert-Judge/Panel as pure agon. Fill sources: Total-Victory exchanges, Belief-aligned wins (reuse the §9.5 hook), and Practitioner-Weaving spectacle (§9.3). BUT tighten the trigger: instead of a novel 'skip remaining exchanges / force immediate resolution' verb (which has no precedent in the engine and reads as a new resolver behavior), route the fervor spike through the EXISTING Persuasion Track exactly as Temporal Axis Conflict already does (§9.4 / PP-351 routes off-axis co-movement to a ±Track shift). At threshold, FERVOR fires a large one-shot Track jump toward whichever side the crowd is surging for (GM/disposition-determined, NOT necessarily the leading side — that is the ilinx point: the roar can carry the weaker case), large enough to potentially cross 7/3 and clinch. This emits the same outcome Keys (Decisive/Obligation/Total Victory) with zero new resolution path — it is a clock that writes to the Persuasion Track, both already canonical objects. The 'crowd will not wait' fiction is preserved because a single threshold-Track-jump ends the contest by hitting a win threshold mid-format, without inventing an exchange-skip mechanic.

gaps.title: No Type-3 procedure: consensus/unanimity/sortition exists in the world-fiction but has no contest-locus mechanism
gaps.research_basis: Politics companion D.1 Type 3: procedures that REMOVE competition by design (consensus, sortition). The crucial finding: Type-3 procedures generate a characteristic pathology (the consensus holdout/veto, the captured lottery, the liberum-veto paralysis) and that re-emergence IS the narrative.
gaps.system_state: The contest's only non-Type-1/2 outcome is 'Compromise (4-6): GM narrates partial outcome' and BG's 'referred to committee'; consensus governance is named in the fiction (RM cells, Warden Menders) but has no resolution procedure of its own — it is narrated or routed back through the same Mandate sigma vote.

locations.quote: 4–6 = compromise (GM narrates partial outcome proportional to final position)
Persuasion Track ≥ 7 = motion passes; ≤ 3 = motion fails; 4–6 = referred to committee.
Consensus governance (not hierarchical). Governance stat = average Spirit of active Menders.
node-based meritocratic consensus cells — governance through equity, participation, care, and collaborative decision-making

gaps.critique: RM and the Wardens are EXPLICITLY consensus institutions (Type-3 by ideology), and the Succession adjudicator table even lists 'RM: Mandate ≥ 3 organizers by consensus' — but 'by consensus' is a label with no procedure; it collapses straight back into the Mandate-weighted sigma vote. The system has thus omitted the entire Type-3 design space: there is no unanimity threshold, no single-actor veto/holdout move inside a contest, no sortition draw. Note that downgrading is warranted only partially: the FICTION exists (so this is a seam, not a void), but no CONTEST MECHANIC realizes it.
