gaps.churn_relevance: This is the largest under-configuration on the churn axiom because Type-3 is the research's richest pathology generator. With no Type-3 procedure there is no holdout to game and no captured pool to corrupt — a whole class of emergent-narrative seeds is simply unreachable.
gaps.improvement: Add a Type-3 'Consensus Proceeding' as a thin variant of the existing track, not a new subsystem. (a) UNANIMITY: replace the 0–10 majority track with a per-participant assent flag; the proceeding resolves only when all required assenters flip. Each holdout's refusal is a single sigma roll (their will + advantage vs the proposer) — reuse the Argue resolver. (b) HOLDOUT-AS-CHURN: a lone holdout who blocks does not terminate the scene; it mints a 'Holdout' Obligation clock (reuse §6.1) binding the coalition to the holdout's named condition, AND generates a Scene Slate entry next season (reuse §6.3 chain hook). (c) ANTI-GAMING ANTIBODY: import the historical Iroquois anti-frivolous-objection / Venetian anti-bribery analogue — a holdout who blocks without a Conviction-aligned reason (armature dot-product of the motion against their Conviction below threshold) takes a Mandate/Disposition cost, exactly mirroring the existing 'self-interested Sacred Veto costs Mandate −1' antibody in faction_layer §5.3. (d) SORTITION: a Succession or panel-adjudicator variant that draws the decider from the eligible pool by a single d10 draw (sigma-B, the bare-stat/drawn kernel the distillation report already names), with the captured-pool pathology surfacing when one faction has pre-stacked the eligible list.

verdict.evidence_quote: Succession adjudicator row: "RM: Mandate ≥ 3 organizers by consensus" (social_contest_v30.md §7.2); and the only non-decisive contest outcomes are "4–6 = compromise (GM narrates partial outcome proportional to final position)" (§6) / "4–6 = referred to committee" (§10) — vote thresholds in faction_layer_v30 §5.3 are exclusively "Majority | Strictly > 50% of participating Mandate" and "Supermajority | ≥ 60% of participating Mandate". No assent/unanimity/sortition row exists anywhere.
verdict.reasoning: I confirmed all four cited quotes verbatim (§6 compromise line, §10 referred-to-committee line, campaign_architecture §6.3 "Consensus governance (not hierarchical). Governance stat = average Spirit of active Menders", PART 6 "node-based meritocratic consensus cells"). The Type-3 gap is real: I grepped designs/, params/, and faction_layer for consensus|unanimity|veto|sortition|holdout|assent|quorum. Every consensus hit is FICTION (RM cells, Warden Menders) or a STAT (average Spirit governance value), never a contest resolver. Every actual contest/vote resolution primitive is a 0–10 majority track (§6/§10) or a Mandate-share Majority/Supermajority threshold (faction_layer §5.3). The Succession table's "by consensus" label is the strongest proof: it sits in the adjudicator column and then collapses straight into the same Mandate-weighted §3-§5 Grand Contest pipeline — there is no per-participant assent flag, no single-actor holdout/veto move INSIDE a contest, and no sortition draw. The closest existing veto (Church Sacred Veto, faction_layer §5.3) is a faction-scale BG motion-cancel, NOT a contest-locus holdout, which actually corroborates the gap. This is a seam not a void (the fiction exists), so the partial-downgrade framing in the critique is honest, but the missing design space is large and the leverage stays high under the churn axiom: with no holdout there is nothing to game (liberum-veto paralysis) and no captured pool to corrupt — a whole pathology class is unreachable.
verdict.refined_improvement: Keep the four sub-mechanics but bind them harder to named existing primitives so this stays a thin variant, not a subsystem. (a) UNANIMITY/ASSENT: add a "Consensus Proceeding" flag to §2 Step 1 adjudicator types (alongside the provisional Panel/ED-137 slot); resolve not on the 0–10 track but on a per-required-assenter flag, each holdout's refusal being one Argue roll (sigma-A: their Cha/Cog/Att-primary pool + advantage vs proposer, §3/§4 unchanged). (b) HOLDOUT-AS-CHURN: a lone sustained holdout mints a "Holdout" Obligation clock via the EXISTING §6.1 machinery (clock_registry, named verifiable condition) and a §6.3 Scene-Slate chain entry next season — no new clock type. (c) ANTI-GAMING ANTIBODY: gate frivolous blocking on the armature dot-product already named in the distillation report — if the motion's dot-product against the holdout's Conviction (conviction_taxonomy_v30 13-Conviction/4-axis matrix) is below threshold, the block costs Mandate/Disposition, structurally mirroring faction_layer §5.3's confirmed "self-interested veto is transparent; reputation cost" (Mandate −1). (d) SORTITION: a Succession/panel variant that draws the decider by a single d10 via sigma-B (the distillation report's explicitly-named bare-stat/drawn kernel: "domain_actions ... piety crisis d6 (castable)"), with the captured-pool pathology surfacing when one faction pre-stacks the eligible Standing ≥ 5 list (§7.2). Tighten one thing the original glossed: the antibody should reuse the sigma-A Argue resolver's degree bands for the holdout's block roll rather than inventing a new pass/fail, so the holdout's strength of objection (not just yes/no) feeds the Mandate cost.

gaps.title: Procedural pathology is configured as a TERMINATOR (stall-killer), the inverse of pathology-as-churn
gaps.research_basis: Politics companion D.1: the deadlocked/abolished competition re-emerging is the emergent narrative; the richest churn is a procedure whose FAILURE MODE seeds the next arc, not one that resolves the stall.
gaps.system_state: Deadlock, the chain hard-stop, and the 3-Compromise 'cold equilibrium' are all written as devices that exhaust patience and FORCE closure: zero-movement → resistance drops → third zero-movement ends as Compromise; three Compromises freeze Disposition and lock the topic for 4 seasons.

locations.quote: If a third consecutive zero-movement exchange occurs after the Resistance drop, the contest ends immediately as a Compromise at the current track position.

After 3 consecutive Compromises, the tension resolves as a permanent stalemate: both parties establish a cold equilibrium. Disposition freezes at current level. Neither party can initiate a contest on this topic for 4 seasons.
Rationale: prolonged mutual stonewalling exhausts the audience's patience. The institutional pressure forces resolution or capitulation.
After 10 consecutive exchanges without resolution: forced Unmask fires.

gaps.critique: These rules fight the churn axiom directly. The research says deadlock is a STORY ENGINE — the holdout weaponizing paralysis for leverage, the liberum veto that becomes the plot — but Valoria treats every stall as a bug to be drained off by automatic resistance erosion and a 4-season topic lockout. The 'cold equilibrium' is the single most anti-churn line in the spec: it freezes Disposition (no relational drift) AND bars re-contest (no follow-up scene), i.e. it converts a deadlock into INERTNESS, the exact thing the memory's churn principle says to suspect. Contrast FG-2, where a hung procedure productively bifurcates the map — §6.3 should have done the same and instead does the opposite.
gaps.churn_relevance: Direct violation: a deadlock that locks the topic for 4 seasons and freezes Disposition generates the minimum possible churn. The procedure's most dramatic failure mode (irreducible disagreement) is configured to produce silence rather than the next arc.
gaps.improvement: Re-point the terminators at generation, reusing existing buckets. (a) Replace the 4-season cold-equilibrium LOCKOUT with a persistent 'Deadlock' Obligation clock (reuse §6.1): the unresolved topic stays live as a standing tension that any party can spend Momentum/Mandate to re-open, and that ticks a Disposition-drift effect each Accounting instead of freezing it. (b) Make the holdout a PLAYABLE leverage move: a party who deliberately sustains zero-movement (stonewalling) converts accumulated audience impatience into a one-time concession demand — i.e. deadlock becomes a bargaining chip with a sigma-resolved success, not an auto-Compromise. (c) Keep an institutional pressure-release valve but make IT the churn: 'audience patience exhausted' should escalate the scene (a third party seizes the initiative, a Succession-style split, a Chamber Violence forfeit) rather than quietly downgrading to Compromise. Each reuses the track, the Obligation clock, and the Scene Slate hook already present.

verdict.reasoning:
The narrow target is real but the framing and leverage are inflated. Two things are true at once:

(1) The specific "cold equilibrium" line IS anti-churn as written. social_contest_v30.md §6.3 line 311: after 3 Compromises, "Disposition freezes at current level. Neither party can initiate a contest on this topic for 4 seasons." A simultaneous Disposition-freeze + topic-lockout does convert a deadlock into inertness, and that is the one line worth re-pointing. The finding's quotes all verify verbatim.

(2) BUT the finding's claim that the system "treats every stall as a bug to be drained off" and that "the procedure's most dramatic failure mode is configured to produce silence rather than the next arc" is refuted by the surrounding corpus and even by §6.3 itself:

- §6.3 is literally titled "Chain Contests (NEW — unresolved tension generates follow-up)" and opens (line 298): "When a contest ends in Compromise (Persuasion Track 4–6), the tension is deferred, not resolved. The unresolved contest generates a Scene Slate entry for the following season per player_agency_v30 §4.2 (Priority 1 — the unresolved political tension is a crisis event)." So a stall is the canonical churn-seed here. The cold-equilibrium terminator only fires AFTER the chain has already spawned 2–3 follow-up scenes (max chain length 3). It is a tractability bound on an already-churning loop, not a stall-killer applied to a single deadlock.

- Deadlock-as-story-engine already exists at the adjacent scale. arcs_16_19.md Arc 18 "The Tied Vote": a hung Parliamentary Vote is NOT terminated — "tie = motion fails by abstention → Church Influence +1 AND Mending Stability −1 simultaneously," and "The arc after the tied vote is about what comes next, which is always worse than what came before... Church Influence +1 means the Church is one season closer to the seizure threshold." This is exactly the "failure mode seeds the next arc" pattern the finding says is missing.

- The finding's improvement (b) ("Make the holdout a PLAYABLE leverage move") proposes inventing something the corpus already has. npc_behavior_v30.md §5 Edeyja "Arc A: The Holdout" is a holdout whose stasis is NOT freeze-inert — "The Southernmost slowly loses ground. MS decline accelerates" (it ticks a drift effect, the very thing improvement (a) asks for). And "Arc E: The Wager" is a playable Grand-Contest leverage move with a sigma-resolved (Track) outcome that turns sustained refusal into a bargaining instrument — precisely improvement (b).

So the leverage is not high: one anti-churn line inside an otherwise churn-generating section, in a system that demonstrably models deadlock-as-engine elsewhere. Correct leverage is low-to-medium.
verdict.evidence_quote: When a contest ends in Compromise (Persuasion Track 4–6), the tension is deferred, not resolved. The unresolved contest generates a Scene Slate entry for the following season per player_agency_v30 §4.2 (Priority 1 — the unresolved political tension is a crisis event).
verdict.refined_improvement: Scope the fix to the single offending line (the §6.3 max-chain-3 cold-equilibrium terminator); do not re-architect the whole §6.3 chain (it already seeds follow-up via Scene Slate on every Compromise) and do not invent a holdout subsystem (Edeyja Arc A Holdout + Arc E Wager already model the drift-not-freeze holdout and the playable-leverage deadlock). Minimal change, reusing existing buckets: when a chain hits 3 Compromises, instead of "Disposition freezes + topic locked 4 seasons," (a) emit the topic as a standing Deadlock Obligation clock per §6.1 (which already tracks via clock_registry and already supports any party spending to re-open) so the topic stays live, and (b) drop the Disposition freeze — let the existing per-Accounting Disposition drift continue, mirroring the Arc 18 Tied Vote pattern (deadlock ticks adjacent clocks: e.g. a small Mandate/Stability drift or Doubt accumulation toward the more-resistant party) rather than zeroing all relational movement. Keep an institutional pressure-release valve but route it to the Scene Slate as a third-party-seizes-initiative or Succession-style split (§7.2), reusing the hooks §6.3 already calls. No new resolver — the contest's existing Track/sigma kernel and the §6.1 Obligation clock cover all of it.

gaps.title: Anti-gaming antibodies exist elsewhere in the engine but are absent from the contest's own procedures
gaps.research_basis: Politics companion D.1: constitutions build anti-gaming antibodies (Iroquois anti-frivolous-objection rule; Venetian anti-bribery oaths) precisely because the procedure can be gamed — the antibody presupposes and is shaped by the expected pathology.
gaps.system_state: The faction layer has a working antibody (self-interested Sacred Veto costs extra Mandate) and proposals model 'Patrician Capture' of committees, but the contest's own procedures (Corroboration, Coalition shared pool, lobbying offset, Recall citation) have caps and clamps tuned for BALANCE, not antibodies tuned against a named gaming pathology.

locations.quote: If Church uses Sacred Veto to block a motion that protects Church interests: additional Mandate −1 (self-interested veto is transparent; reputation cost).
committee competence shifts toward serving merchant clan interests; Parliamentary actions cost +1 Ob unless aligned with named clan agenda
Lobbying cannot predetermine a vote — it provides advantage, not a guaranteed outcome.
Max positional bonus: +5D (genre +1, audience +1, MS targeting +1, Recall +2). Each prerequisite independently costly.

gaps.critique: The contest is full of BALANCE caps (lobby clamp to 4–6, +2D Findings cap, +5D stacking cap, Grand-Contest Recall exhaustion) — these prevent dominance but they are not antibodies in the research sense: they do not presuppose a gaming PATHOLOGY and turn its detection into churn. The faction layer shows the engine knows how to build a real antibody (the Sacred Veto self-interest cost makes the gaming visible and costly, generating reputation churn). The contest should carry an equivalent: a move to detect and price the GAMED procedure (a coalition stacked purely for pool-math; a holdout with no Conviction basis), with the detection itself producing a Suspicion/Disposition consequence rather than a silent cap.
gaps.churn_relevance: Medium-high: a cap that silently truncates a pool generates no story; an antibody that EXPOSES the gamer (Suspicion Token, Mandate cost, Belief formation 'they argue in bad faith') generates relational churn and feeds the armature. The current config chooses the inert option.
gaps.improvement: Convert one or two contest caps into antibodies reusing the existing Suspicion Token and the armature. Example: a Corroboration/Coalition assembled where corroborators' Convictions do NOT align with the motion (armature dot-product below threshold) is flagged as 'bad-faith bloc' — instead of (or in addition to) the pool cap, place a Suspicion Token on the bloc leader and let the adjudicator's Conviction Scar check fire, mirroring §9.4b's Certainty-indexed adjudicator response. This reuses the Sanction-style Suspicion mechanic already in contest_extensions and the §9.4b response table, so it is a re-point, not a new subsystem.

verdict.evidence_quote: CR5 | Orientations re-grounded: Direct -> moves Persuasion (merits); Indirect -> attacks opponent Face (ethos), with self-Face backfire on failure | Doubt Marker / Suspicion Token | F7 self-gating: obstruction is bounded by your own standing (Quintilian/Nyaya)
verdict.reasoning:
The cited quotes all verify exactly (faction_layer_v30.md:434 Sacred Veto self-interest cost; PC-4.4 Patrician Capture; social_contest §10 line 500 lobby clamp; contest_extensions line 57 +5D stacking cap). The descriptive core of the finding is correct FOR THE LIVE v30 SPEC: the contest's own caps (lobby 4-6, +2D Findings, +5D stacking, Grand-Contest Recall exhaustion) are balance clamps, not pathology-shaped antibodies, and the one in-contest Suspicion mechanic (Sanction MS targeting, contest_extensions:47 "Suspicion Token placed regardless of outcome") is a tactic-level marker, not a detector of a GAMED procedure. So the finding is not refuted. But it is overstated/under-researched on two counts that drop leverage to low.

(1) The engine already HAS a research-sense bad-faith antibody, just not inside the contest module: the Sincerity Gate (designs/scene/fieldwork_v30.md §5.3 / fieldwork_v30_infill.md:92 "Consciousness renders other consciousnesses; people perceive bad faith"; investigation_systems_v30.md:308 "[INSTRUMENTAL]-tagged utterances trigger the gate"). It fires on purely-instrumental engagement and produces a Disposition consequence — the exact antibody-presupposes-pathology shape D.1 describes. This does not refute the finding (the gate is in fieldwork/dialogue, not the contest) but it means the pattern, the Suspicion mechanic, and the precedent already exist, so the improvement is a re-point, not net-new design.

(2) More decisively, the antibody the finding asks for has ALREADY BEEN DESIGNED INTO THE CONTEST'S OWN PROCEDURES. The 2026-06-01 ground-up redesign (designs/audit/2026-06-01-contest-redesign/RATIFIED_2026-06-01.md), ratified by Jordan as provisional canon-of-record, decision CR5: "Indirect -> attacks opponent Face (ethos), with self-Face backfire on failure ... F7 self-gating: obstruction is bounded by your own standing (Quintilian/Nyaya)." The omega vetting block explicitly lists "F7 self-gating prevent a dominant strategy" as a design pillar. This is precisely a named-pathology antibody: gamed/frivolous obstruction recoils onto the obstructor's own Face/standing rather than being silently clamped. The finding cites only the live v30 caps and never engages with CR5/F7. The gap is therefore real only in the UNPROPAGATED live spec — the redesign already closes it. Leverage drops to low: this is a propagation/wiring task, not an undiscovered design gap.
verdict.refined_improvement: Reframe from "convert caps into antibodies" (net-new) to "propagate the already-ratified F7 self-gating antibody (CR5, RATIFIED_2026-06-01) into the live social_contest_v30 spec, and extend it from per-move Face-backfire to bloc-level bad-faith detection." Concretely: when a Coalition/Corroboration bloc is assembled whose corroborators' armature_position fails the dot-product alignment test against the motion's symbolic_dimensions (key_substrate_v30.md:153 npc.armature_position[axis] x key.symbolic_dimensions[axis] x impact_vector below threshold), apply the CR5 self-Face backfire to the bloc leader instead of (or with) the §9.2 shared-pool effect, and place the existing Sanction Suspicion Token (contest_extensions:47) on the leader, optionally firing the §9.4b Certainty-indexed adjudicator response. This reuses three existing primitives — F7 self-gating (CR5), the armature dot-product (key_substrate), and the Sanction Suspicion Token — so it is pure re-point with zero new subsystem, and it honors the engine's two-primitive discipline. Note the dependency: this work should ride the broader CR1-CR7 propagation, not land as a standalone bolt-on ahead of it.

gaps.title: The procedure is hard-coded at GM setup; choice-of-venue is not a contestable, churn-generating move
gaps.research_basis: Politics Part D.4 + Hart's rule of recognition: the deepest political act is choosing WHICH GAME legitimates power — a meta-game. Florence/Venice: the same procedural materials yield wildly different outcomes by who controls the framing (accoppiatori choosing the draw a mano vs a mano stretta).
gaps.system_state: Adjudicator type, exchange count, role structure, resistance, and faction boost are all fixed by the GM before the contest and cannot change during it; players merely learn the format.

locations.quote: The adjudicator type is **fixed for the duration of a contest.** If circumstances change enough to shift the adjudicator type (a private negotiation goes public), the current contest ends and a new contest begins under the new type.
The Game Master (GM) sets the format at setup; players know it before the contest begins.
These dice are added to the Argue pool at Step 3 of each exchange. They are fixed at setup — no mid-contest changes.

gaps.critique: The single most consequential variable in any historical deliberation — under whose rules, before whom, in what genre — is removed from player agency and frozen by fiat. A petitioner who could shift a Crown Audience (Expert Judge, Cognition, halved-resistance-for-petitioner) into a public Crowd contest (Charisma, full resistance) is making the exact accoppiatori move the research centers; the spec instead ends the contest and silently re-instantiates it, dropping the maneuver on the floor. The genre table (Memory vs Projection) is derived mechanically 'from the question' rather than being a thing factions fight to define — yet whether the question is 'did X happen' or 'what should follow' IS the political fight.
gaps.churn_relevance: This is the richest churn the system forgoes: a procedure whose FRAMING is contestable generates capture, forum-shopping, and legitimacy disputes — exactly the failure-modes-as-story the churn axiom prizes. Freezing it at setup converts the highest-leverage decision into invisible GM bookkeeping, producing zero churn.
gaps.improvement: Add a pre-contest 'Procedure Contest' as a single sigma-A exchange BEFORE Step 1: each side rolls (relevant Attribute×2)+History to set ONE setup parameter (adjudicator type, primary genre, or proceeding type) toward its preferred value; winner's margin sets it, ties go to the institution's default. Reuse the existing Persuasion-Track/margin math; no new subsystem. Make genre-of-record a stake the loser of this pre-round must accept. The displaced-contest case (private→public) becomes a player-triggerable Venue Shift move, not an automatic GM reset.

verdict.evidence_quote: Escalation between social modes (negotiation → debate → appeal) | Conceptually identified, not designed
verdict.reasoning:
All three cited quotes verify verbatim in the working tree: social_contest_v30.md §2 Step 1 ("The adjudicator type is **fixed for the duration of a contest.** If circumstances change enough to shift the adjudicator type (a private negotiation goes public), the current contest ends and a new contest begins under the new type."), §2 Step 3 ("These dice are added to the Argue pool at Step 3 of each exchange. They are fixed at setup — no mid-contest changes."), and infill line 19 ("The Game Master (GM) sets the format at setup; players know it before the contest begins."). I grepped the whole corpus (designs/, params/, references/) for venue/forum-shop/procedure-contest/choose-adjudicator/frame-the-question mechanics and found NONE. The only adjacent mechanic is the very auto-reset the finding attacks: "the current contest ends and a new contest begins under the new type" — confirming the displaced-venue maneuver is dropped on the floor rather than rewarded as a player move. The critique is independently corroborated by the spec's OWN backlog: social_contest_v30.md §12 "Future design work (not blocking)" lists "Escalation between social modes (negotiation → debate → appeal) | Conceptually identified, not designed" and "Appraise step expansion (reveal Beliefs, Knot vulnerabilities) | Conceptually identified, not specified" — i.e. the framing-mobility gap is an acknowledged-but-unbuilt item, not handled elsewhere. The genre-derivation claim also checks out: §2 Step 2 mechanically reads genre "from the question" via a fixed table, with infill §2 confirming "If the question does not clearly favour one genre, the GM assigns" — genre-of-record is never a contestable stake. On engine discipline, the proposed fix is sound: the distillation report (distillation_coherence_report.md §1, line 201) confirms "social_contest exchange" IS sigma-A, so a pre-contest "Procedure Contest" run as one sigma-A exchange reusing Persuasion-Track/margin math adds no new resolver primitive — it is the same kernel applied one layer earlier.

Honest caveats that keep leverage at high rather than maximal: (1) the system is NOT "zero churn" overall — it already models rich post-resolution framing churn (§6.1 Obligations, §6.3 Chain Contests, §7.2 Succession faction-split, §7.3 Heresy lifecycle); the gap is narrowly the PRE-contest framing layer, so "produces zero churn" overstates by ignoring downstream churn. (2) A partial pre-contest contestation already exists: §10/§11 lobbying offset (PP-256/ED-621) lets prior Diplomacy actions shift the STARTING Persuasion Track within the 4-6 compromise zone — so pre-contest positional contestation is precedented, which both validates the design pattern and slightly narrows the novelty (the gap is procedure/genre framing, not pre-contest contestation in general). Net: the gap is real, high-leverage, and the richest under-exploited churn source in the system, but its severity is "the highest-leverage missing piece" not "the system generates zero churn."
verdict.refined_improvement: Adopt the proposed pre-contest "Procedure Contest" but scope and anchor it tighter so it reuses existing structure and doesn't bolt on a subsystem: (1) Run it as a SINGLE sigma-A exchange (the contest exchange is already sigma-A per distillation_coherence_report §1) before §2 Step 1, using the standard Argue pool and §4 CLASH margin math — no new resolver. (2) Limit the contestable parameter to genre-of-record and/or adjudicator type only (NOT exchange count or faction boost, which are institution-structural); the winner's margin-over-resistance sets the disputed parameter toward its preferred value, ties default to the institution's standing format. (3) Reuse the §10/§11 lobbying-offset precedent for legitimacy: just as Diplomacy actions already pre-shift the starting Track, the Procedure Contest pre-shifts the framing — fold it under the same "pre-contest contestation" umbrella rather than presenting it as novel. (4) Replace the §2-Step-1 automatic "private→public = new contest" reset with a player-triggerable Venue Shift move: declaring it costs the mover a forfeited first exchange (Regroup-equivalent) in the new venue, so forum-shopping has a price and generates capture/legitimacy churn rather than being free or impossible. Make genre-of-record an explicit §2 Step 6 stake the loser of the Procedure Contest must accept, so the framing loss is recorded as a tracked consequence.

gaps.title: No adjudicator-capture lever: Disposition and Subversion exist but never reach inside the contest
gaps.research_basis: The Medici did not abolish the lottery; they CAPTURED it — loaded the Borsellino, controlled the accoppiatori, bypassed the draw. Capture of the mechanism, not victory within it, is the empirical finding.
gaps.system_state: The adjudicator is a neutral fixed function; Disposition toward the adjudicator and the Subversion Domain Action (which lowers a target NPC's Disposition) exist in the engine but have no documented effect on contest resolution.

locations.quote: | Type | Who decides | Examples | Primary attribute |
| Subversion | Faction wants to destabilize rival | Reduce target territory Accord or target NPC Disposition | Player's cover blown; Exposure consequences |
a Standing 4+ character can call a leadership challenge at any time via social contest against the current leader, with the faction council as expert adjudicator (Cognition-based).

gaps.critique: The corpus already has every primitive for capture — Disposition (−5..+5), the Subversion DA, faction Mandate, the Renaissance-anchored intelligence-broker NPCs noted in the integration proposal — but they stop at the chamber door. An adjudicating council the player has cultivated to Disposition +3 adjudicates identically to one at −3. The leadership-challenge instance even hard-names 'the faction council as expert adjudicator' with no path to pack, recuse, or sway it. This is the difference between modeling capture and modeling a fair trial. Note this is an ABSENCE confirmed by grep, not an overlooked mechanic: Subversion can move an NPC's Disposition but nothing converts that Disposition into adjudicator bias.
gaps.churn_relevance: A capturable adjudicator turns every prior season of relationship-building and subversion into a deferred, revealed-at-resolution payoff — and its failure mode (a capture exposed mid-contest) is premium scandal churn. Currently that investment is inert at the moment it should matter most.
gaps.improvement: Bind adjudicator Disposition to resistance and to a capped boost: adjudicator Disposition toward a side modifies that side's resistance by floor(Disp/2) (so cultivated favor = a real, bounded thumb on the scale, mirroring the Lobby Cap philosophy). Add an 'Expose Capture' Appraise check (reuse the §4 Step 1 Appraise pool, Overwhelming) that, on success, voids the captured adjudicator's bias and fires Reputation/Disposition fallout — making capture high-value-but-fragile, not a guaranteed win. For panels, let Subversion DA recuse or seat one juror pre-contest. All reuses existing Disposition + Appraise + resistance primitives.

verdict.reasoning: The core gap is real and verified by grep across the live corpus. (1) The adjudicator is a fixed neutral function: social_contest_v30 §2 Step 1 sets adjudicator TYPE (which only selects the primary attribute) and §2 Step 4 derives resistance purely from "average Stability of represented factions, round up, then −1" — no Disposition term. (2) sim/personal/contest.py confirms this in code: `resistance` is sourced only from `stakes.get('resistance', RESISTANCE_DEFAULT)` (§2 audience resistance) and `movement = max(0, margin - resistance)`; there is no adjudicator-Disposition input anywhere in resolve_exchange/resolve_contest. (3) social_contest_v30_infill.md confirms Disposition fires only as an OUTPUT/consequence of Total Victory ("Disposition and Reputation shifts fire from Total Victory"), never as an input to resolution. (4) The Subversion DA exists and moves NPC Disposition (player_agency_v30 §3.3 line 143) but nothing converts that Disposition into adjudicator bias — the conversion-to-institutional-bias idea is in fact explicitly logged as an UNFILLED gap G-7 in designs/audit/2026-04-28-political-dynamics-session/01_interpersonal_audit.md ("Intelligence lacks active influence tier ... new social action to degrade rival NPC's Disposition with their own leader"), corroborating that this is an absence, not an overlooked mechanic. (5) The leadership-challenge instance is verbatim and confirms the design hard-names a neutral adjudicating council with no pack/recuse/sway path. Two caveats keep this from being a slam-dunk "high": (a) the corpus DOES already route "cultivated political favor → bounded pre-resolution advantage" via the Lobby Cap (ED-621, social_contest §10/§11), so the novel surface is specifically the TTRPG-personal expert/panel adjudicator, not adjudicator-favor-as-a-whole; (b) the finding's research_basis overclaims "Renaissance-anchored intelligence-broker NPCs noted in the integration proposal" — that phrase is not locatable in integration_proposal_v30.md (the only adjudicator mentions there treat it as a style-preference to be Appraised, not a capturable target). That overclaim is peripheral; the central mechanical gap stands.
verdict.evidence_quote: Audience resistance: average Stability of represented factions, round up, then −1 (minimum 0). Typical range: 0–2.
verdict.refined_improvement: Keep the proposal but scope and channel it precisely: (1) Bind adjudicator Disposition into the EXISTING resistance channel rather than adding a parallel modifier — the §2 Step 4 resistance formula and the per-exchange erosion (ED-864/ED-295 Option D) are the single resolution knob, so add: "For Expert-judge and Panel adjudicator types, adjudicator Disposition toward a side reduces THAT side's effective resistance by floor(Disp/2), capped at the existing Lobby-Cap-style bound so it cannot predetermine the outcome (mirrors ED-621: advantage, not a guaranteed win)." This reuses the sigma-kernel resistance term the sim already computes; no new subsystem. (2) "Expose Capture" should explicitly reuse the §4 Step 1 Appraise pool (Attunement + Recall, Ob = adjudicator Cha/2) at Overwhelming, voiding the bias and firing Reputation/Disposition fallout — making capture high-value-but-fragile. (3) For Panels, gate the Subversion DA to a pre-contest recuse/seat action against ONE juror, consuming the DA and leaving an Exposure trail per its existing failure clause. (4) Pin the improvement to the unresolved ED-137 Panel-mechanics stub and the G-7 audit gap so it lands as a closure, not a bolt-on. Drop the "intelligence-broker NPCs noted in the integration proposal" justification — it is not corroborated by integration_proposal_v30.md.

gaps.title: No robust-action / multivocality lever: commit-legibly vs stay-ambiguous is collapsed into a binary style die
gaps.research_basis: Cosimo won by ROBUST ACTION + MULTIVOCALITY — noncommittal moves that preserved his options while narrowing opponents'; one action legible from many angles. Option-value preservation, not maximal force, was the winning strategy.
gaps.system_state: Each exchange forces a single fully-specified style (genre + Revealing/Obscuring); Obscuring merely converts a win into a Doubt Marker. There is no stance that trades immediate margin for preserved positional ambiguity / future option-value.

locations.quote: Genre + orientation presented as a single choice per exchange:
Obscuring in CROSS: if the side with larger movement used Obscuring, place a Doubt Marker on the opponent instead of track movement.
Each orator selects a genre (Memory or Projection) and an orientation (Revealing or Obscuring) as a single style pick

gaps.critique: Obscuring is configured as a tactical damage-reroute (margin → Doubt Marker), not as Cosimo-style multivocality: a move whose meaning stays plural so that whichever way the contest breaks, the actor remains aligned with the winning side. The system makes you commit to a genre AND orientation every exchange — maximal legibility — which is precisely the posture robust action avoids. There is no mechanic for declining to fully commit in order to keep options open, nor any reward for an argument that reads favorably to multiple factions on a mixed panel.
gaps.churn_relevance: Multivocality is the engine of the most interesting political narratives — the ally who was never quite committed, the speech everyone heard differently. A lever that lets a player bank ambiguity for later realignment seeds betrayal/reversal arcs; forcing full commitment every exchange flattens deliberation into a damage race and suppresses that churn.
gaps.improvement: Add an 'Equivocal' stance as a third orientation option in §4 Step 2: it forfeits this exchange's track movement but places a token on the Persuasion Track that the player may, at any later exchange, declare as having argued the now-winning side's genre — retroactively claiming alignment (capped: one Equivocal token per contest, expires at contest end if unspent). On a mixed/panel adjudicator, an Equivocal argument counts its audience-boost die against the most-favorable juror. Pure reuse of the existing style-pick + Doubt-Marker token machinery and the deferred-resolution pattern already used by Wager Obligations.

verdict.evidence_quote: Obscuring in CROSS: if the side with larger movement used Obscuring, place a Doubt Marker on the opponent instead of track movement.
verdict.reasoning:
All three cited quotes verified verbatim and in-context: params/contest.md:35 ("Genre + orientation presented as a single choice per exchange"), social_contest_v30.md:135 (genre+orientation as "a single style pick"), and social_contest_v30.md:170 (the CROSS Obscuring→Doubt Marker clause). The system-state claim holds: Obscuring's ENTIRE function is to convert a win into a margin-debuff token — in CLASH/REINFORCE an Obscuring win deals no track movement and places a Doubt Marker (−2 to opponent's next winning margin; one active; consumed on use), and CROSS reroutes the same way (social_contest §4 Step 4; params Interaction Types). There is no posture that trades margin for preserved positional ambiguity / future realignment.

Already-handled check (the corpus is large, so I grepped hard): a case-insensitive scan of all of designs/ for multivocal|robust action|option-value|equivocal|noncommittal|plural|hedge|defer-align returns ZERO hits in the social space — only unrelated "ambiguous Mending outcome" (threadwork) and tooling "disambiguation." The adjacent systems do not fill the gap: (a) Resonant Style (npc_behavior_v30 §1.3, contest_extensions "Resonant Style Targeting") is a vulnerability-TARGETING axis that rewards fully-committed, precisely-aimed arguments — the inverse of robust action; (b) the Persuasion Track is bidirectional but is a shared position clock, not a personal option-value store; (c) Wager Obligations (social_contest §6.1) defer future CONTENT (a verifiable condition owed by the loser), not alignment ambiguity — so they are a legitimate structural precedent for "deferred resolution" without already covering multivocality. The gap is real.

Leverage held at medium (not higher): the contest IS the sigma kernel (distillation_coherence_report §1 + Appendix A) and the engine's do-not-touch list praises the existing Appraise-first / bidirectional-track shape, so this is one optional orientation among four, gated one-per-contest — high value for panel/political scenes, marginal in the common 1–3-exchange dyad. Churn rationale (the never-quite-committed ally, the speech everyone heard differently seeding betrayal/reversal arcs) is genuine.
verdict.refined_improvement: Add an "Equivocal" orientation (3rd option at §4 Step 2) that forfeits this exchange's track movement and places an Equivocal token as ACTOR-STATE (modeled on the existing Doubt Marker / Suspicion Token, NOT parked on the Persuasion Track — the track is a shared bidirectional clock per distillation §6, so a private realignment token must live in actor state to respect the bucket model). Cap: one per contest; expires unspent at contest end. Resolution at post-contest accounting (reuse the §6.1 deferred-resolution pattern, not a mid-contest track mutation): if held to the end, the orator may declare alignment with the winning side's genre, taking the Disposition/Reputation/Obligation consequence of that side rather than their nominal one — banking ambiguity for later realignment. DROP the original's "audience-boost die against the most-favorable juror" clause: Panel adjudication is explicitly undesigned (ED-137, provisional=Expert Judge), so a juror-selection effect cannot be specified yet. Pure reuse of existing token machinery + the Wager deferred-resolution pattern; no new resolver and no track-bucket violation.

gaps.title: The antechamber/broglio is reduced to a +1-per-DA scalar; off-table maneuver is not a play space
gaps.research_basis: Venice did not eliminate maneuver; it CHANNELED it — the broglio: electioneering on the stairways, 'big fish eat the smaller', the Signoria 'helpless to stop the lobbying'. Fiat DISPLACES contest to the antechamber: the most absolutist polities generate the most baroque court politics.
gaps.system_state: Off-table influence enters the contest only as a starting-position offset: +1 per successful Diplomacy Domain Action in the preceding season, capped to the 4–6 compromise zone, and only in BG/Hybrid mode.

locations.quote: Each successful Diplomacy action targeting this vote in preceding season: +1 toward lobbying side (max ±2).
Lobbying cannot predetermine a vote — it provides advantage, not a guaranteed outcome.
| Diplomacy | Faction needs alliance or treaty | Improve Disposition with target NPC to threshold | Diplomatic window closes; NPC AI acts independently |

gaps.critique: CREDIT: the Lobby Cap is genuinely well-configured against the research — it models maneuver as advantage-not-fiat, exactly Venice channeling rather than eliminating the broglio, and the +1-per-DA loop correctly makes prior-season effort matter. But it is the ONLY antechamber mechanic, it is a flat scalar, and it is absent from personal-scale TTRPG contests. There is no vote-soliciting minigame, no 'big fish eat smaller' coalition-poaching off the table, no antechamber where a fiat-decided matter (a Crown that simply rules) displaces into baroque side-maneuver. The research's strongest claim — fiat displaces, not eliminates — is unmodeled: a Royal Audience the Crown wins by structural advantage produces no antechamber churn.
gaps.churn_relevance: An antechamber layer is pure churn-by-design: it gives losers of the formal game a place to keep playing, which is where the next arc germinates. Collapsing it to a capped scalar means the off-table political life of the world — the most generative part — produces at most ±2 on a track and then vanishes.
gaps.improvement: Generalize the Diplomacy-offset into an 'Antechamber' pre-contest phase available at personal scale: each side spends scene actions / Disposition to (a) solicit a wavering NPC's corroboration (convert a neutral into a declared coalition member per §9.2), (b) poach an opponent's announced corroborator via higher Disposition (the 'big fish' move), or (c) for fiat-advantaged proceedings (Royal Audience, Tribunal), open a parallel Antechamber clock whose fill grants the disadvantaged side a one-time resistance halving — modeling displacement-not-elimination. All reuse the corroboration system, Disposition, and the existing clock_registry, keeping the Lobby Cap's advantage-not-fiat ceiling.

verdict.evidence_quote: Lobbying (pre-vote, 1 scene action per target) is where Parliament becomes personal-scale — player persuades individual faction representatives via brief private contests.
verdict.reasoning: The cited quotes are accurate (social_contest_v30.md L498 lobbying offset, L500 ED-621 lobby cap). The CREDIT is sound: the Lobby Cap models maneuver-as-advantage-not-fiat exactly as the research describes Venice channeling rather than eliminating the broglio. But the central negative claim — that off-table maneuver "is not a play space" and there is "no vote-soliciting minigame, no coalition-poaching off the table" — is contradicted by the adjacent corpus. (1) A personal-scale vote-soliciting minigame is explicitly designed: designs/ui/valoria_ui_ux_v4_1.md L620 ("Lobbying (pre-vote, 1 scene action per target) ... player persuades individual faction representatives via brief private contests") and integration_audit_v2 W-02 L342 ("Lobbying opportunity. Social Contest (Charisma-primary, Crowd adjudicator, 1 exchange) against a representative of an undecided faction. On Success: +1 vote weight"). (2) The canonical bridge proposal exists as Parliamentary Intent / H-5: integration_proposal_v30.md L196 — a Diplomacy scene with a neutral faction's representative produces a Positioning Roll bonus and evidence flagged for parliamentary use "mirroring the Contest Corroboration mechanic." (3) Coalition formation AND coalition collapse are mechanized in faction_politics_v30.md: rank perquisites grant the ability to "convene other Bannerets privately — key for coalition-building" (L111) and Seneschal "may convene... (political coalition formation)" (L113); coalition collapse is a demotion trigger ("Disposition with 4+ other Parliamentarians dropping below 0 simultaneously", L169). The corroboration substrate the finding wants to reuse already exists (social_contest_v30.md §1 step 2b L137, §9.2 L449-450). What is genuinely UNmodeled is narrower: (a) the lobbying/coalition layer is scattered across UI/audit/proposal docs and the integration_proposal self-describes its bridge mechanisms as "never... formally canonical" (L11), never consolidated into the contest spec; and (b) the specific "fiat DISPLACES contest to the antechamber" mechanic — a fiat-advantaged proceeding (Royal Audience/Tribunal) spawning antechamber churn for the loser — which I could not locate anywhere. So the critique holds in part but its severity is inflated: the play space largely exists in design, just unconsolidated and missing the displacement sub-loop.
verdict.refined_improvement: Do NOT introduce a new "Antechamber phase" subsystem. Two scoped moves: (1) CONSOLIDATE the already-designed but scattered personal-scale lobbying layer into social_contest_v30.md §10/§11 by canonical reference: the W-02 lobbying scene (ui_ux_v4_1 §6.2; integration_audit_v2 W-02) and Parliamentary Intent (integration_proposal_v30 H-5/§196) are the existing "vote-soliciting minigame" and Diplomacy-positioning loop — promote them from proposal/UI status into the contest spec so the +1-per-DA offset is the FACTION-scale shadow of a personal-scale contest, not the only antechamber mechanic. Coalition-poaching ("big fish eat smaller") already has a substrate: it is the inverse of faction_politics coalition-collapse (Disposition of 4+ coalition members dropping below 0) — wire a pre-contest scene action that uses higher Disposition to flip a declared corroborator (reusing §9.2 coalition declaration + Knot/Disposition), rather than inventing new state. (2) The one genuinely-novel and worth-keeping piece is the displacement loop: for fiat-advantaged proceedings (Excommunication Tribunal §7.1 start-7, Royal Audience), have a decisive structural win open a clock on the clock_registry whose fill grants the loser a one-time scene benefit (e.g. a future contest resistance-halving) — modeling "fiat displaces, not eliminates." Keep this as a clock + existing resistance modifier, not a parallel resolver, so it rides the sigma kernel/Key bus and does not become a bolt-on.

gaps.title: The adjudicator is a flat resistance scalar where the engine already has the primitive to make it an armature
gaps.research_basis: The adjudicator is an AGENT WITH A STANDARD the arguments must satisfy, not a number (deliberation Part V.8). The distillation report defines the armature dot-product as the engine's 'meaning-FOR-AN-OBSERVER' primitive — and the adjudicator is the contest's defining observer.
gaps.system_state: After type selection, the adjudicator's only mechanical contributions to resolution are a scalar resistance (average represented-faction Stability, −1) and a single one-time faction boost die. There is no representation of WHAT the adjudicator is persuaded BY.

locations.quote: Audience resistance: average Stability of represented factions, round up, then −1 (minimum 0). Typical range: 0–2.
The **armature dot-product** turns an *event* into *meaning-for-an-observer* (the interpretation engine: `Σ axis: observer.armature[axis] × key.symbolic_dimensions[axis] × key.impact_vector[axis]`).

npc.armature_position[axis] = Σ over c in Convictions:
    npc.personal_convictions[c] × CONVICTION_AXIS_MATRIX[c][axis]

gaps.critique: Resistance as avg-Stability−1 collapses the adjudicator's deliberative content to a single difficulty number — it answers 'how hard is this room to move' but never 'WHAT moves this room.' The research insists the adjudicator carries a standard arguments must satisfy; the engine already represents exactly that as a 13-Conviction × 4-axis armature with a linear dot-product. An Expert Judge with primary Conviction Precedent SHOULD be moved by Memory+Revealing and resist Projection; a Crown crowd (Virtue) should reward Revealing. Today that is faked with a single +1D faction-boost die rather than computed. The system hard-codes via a scalar what the philosophy says must EMERGE from argument-meets-adjudicator-conviction.
gaps.churn_relevance: This is the central bottom-up-emergent violation in the lens: macro verdict legitimacy is gated by a stat-average threshold instead of emerging from per-move symbolic alignment against the judge's armature. Making it emergent multiplies story: a judge whose Conviction the argument grazed but didn't satisfy is a live narrative seam (a reluctant ruling, a judge who can be re-approached) rather than an inert pass/fail.
gaps.improvement: Reuse the existing primitive verbatim. Give each adjudicator an armature_position (computed by §5 of conviction_axis_matrix from its Convictions, exactly like any NPC). At Step 4, dot-product the chosen argument's style/symbolic dimensions (the Style table already projects Precedent/Suppression/Vision/Insinuation onto genre×orientation, which maps to axes) against adjudicator.armature_position; convert the alignment scalar into resistance DELTA (aligned argument lowers effective resistance by ⌊align⌋, contradicting raises it), reusing the existing per-exchange resistance erosion machinery so no new bucket is added. The faction-boost +1D row becomes the degenerate special case of this dot-product. This is the same kernel Resonant Style Targeting already runs against the opponent (FG-3) — just pointed at the adjudicator.

verdict.evidence_quote: Audience resistance: average Stability of represented factions, round up, then −1 (minimum 0). Typical range: 0–2.
verdict.reasoning: All three cited quotes verify verbatim: §2 Step 4 resistance is the avg-Stability−1 scalar (social_contest_v30.md L75); the armature dot-product / "meaning-for-an-observer" framing is in the distillation report §1 (L41); and the §5 composition rule npc.armature_position[axis] = Σ convictions × matrix is in conviction_axis_matrix_v30.md L209-211. The gap is real and not handled elsewhere. I checked the obvious "already-handled" candidate — the Resonant Style targeting kernel FG-3 invokes (npc_behavior_v30 §1.3/§6.3, mirrored in params/contest_extensions). That kernel IS the per-Conviction symbolic-alignment machinery, BUT it is aimed at the NPC OPPONENT/interlocutor — the named adversary you argue against (Almud, Himlensendt, Vaynard…), not at an impersonal third-party adjudicator. The §6.3 targeting effects modify "the Argue roll" and deal "strain to NPC" — opponent-side outcomes. Nothing in the corpus computes how a Crowd, an Expert Judge distinct from your opponent, or a Panel is *disposed* by an argument's symbolic content; the adjudicator's only resolution-side inputs are the scalar resistance and the §2 Step 3 faction-boost (+1D), a coarse 4-option proxy (one genre OR one orientation per faction) rather than a 13×4 dot-product. I grepped every designs/ doc for adjudicator/audience × armature/conviction: the ONLY place an adjudicator's Conviction enters a contest is §9.4b (ED-667), the Certainty-indexed Thread-use response (a heresy/Scar side-channel), which never touches Persuasion-Track resolution. A prior groundup audit independently named this exact seam (2026-06-03-contest-groundup/EVIDENCE_PRESSURE_VALIDATION.md L24: "a rhetorical flourish needs the audience disposed"), corroborating rather than refuting. One overstatement to flag, not severe: in the asymmetric Tribunal/Royal Audience cases the adjudicator and the NPC opponent collapse into one entity, so RS targeting already grazes adjudicator-conviction there — the genuine gap is specifically the Crowd / third-party Expert Judge / Panel cases. That narrows scope slightly but leverage stays high: those are the canonical Formal/Grand Contest adjudicator types, and macro verdict legitimacy is gated by a stat-average instead of emerging from per-move alignment. Leverage confirmed high.
verdict.refined_improvement: Keep the core proposal (give third-party adjudicators an armature_position computed by conviction_axis_matrix §5, exactly like any NPC; at resolution convert argument-style↔adjudicator-armature alignment into a resistance DELTA via the existing per-exchange erosion machinery, no new bucket). Two engine-discipline tightenings: (1) Scope it to the adjudicator types that are NOT the opponent — Crowd, third-party Expert Judge, Panel. For asymmetric proceedings where adjudicator == NPC opponent (Church Tribunal, Royal Audience), the alignment already routes through Resonant Style targeting against that NPC; do not double-count — gate the new adjudicator-armature term off when adjudicator and opponent are the same actor. (2) The dot-product needs a clean projection from contest style (genre×orientation: Precedent/Vision/Suppression/Insinuation) onto the 4 substrate axes (hierarchical/sacred/instrumental/traditional). That projection does not yet exist and is the real design work — without it the "reuse the primitive verbatim" claim is optimistic. Specify it once as a 4-style×4-axis map (Class B, vetted per PP-674) so faction-boost becomes its degenerate row, then the rest is genuinely free reuse. Close SIM-DEBT-04 in the same pass since this changes adjudicator-side resolution math.

gaps.title: Argument-meaning is dot-producted against the OPPONENT but never against the verdict-declaring adjudicator
gaps.research_basis: Aristotle: the genera are grounded in the JUDGE. Competitive debate tabula-rasa: the judge decides solely on arguments made in the round. The party who DECLARES the outcome is the one whose standard matters — yet the engine aims the armature-meaning machine at the wrong party.
gaps.system_state: Resonant Style Targeting fully implements argument→armature meaning, but exclusively against the opponent NPC's Convictions / Resonant Style; the adjudicator is not a target.

locations.quote: Before Argue roll (Step 3): player declares "targeting Resonant Style." GM confirms match. Requires: (a) argument uses Contest style mapping to the declared MS, (b) content addresses a known Belief, (c) player has previously discovered the MS.
| Resonant Style | The NPC is vulnerable to | Because their Conviction... | Contest mapping |

gaps.critique: The contest already HAS a working armature-dot-product layer (Resonant Style Targeting: argument-style mapped to the target's Conviction vulnerability, with +1D and bonus effects on confirmed alignment). It is simply pointed at the OPPONENT — the party you defeat — and never at the ADJUDICATOR — the party who rules. In tabula-rasa terms the design has built a rich model of persuading your sparring partner and a flat scalar for persuading the actual judge. This is backwards relative to both the research and the engine's own observer-armature framing. The fix is nearly free because the targeting machinery (style→axis mapping, Appraise-revealed armature, confirmed-match bonus) already exists.
gaps.churn_relevance: Pointing the existing dot-product at the adjudicator is the maximally engine-disciplined version of FG-2: it reuses a shipped subsystem rather than bolting on a parallel one. It also reactivates churn — Appraising the JUDGE's convictions (not just the opponent's) becomes a meaningful pre-contest move, generating the recon-and-tailor loop the research says deliberation is about.
gaps.improvement: Generalize Resonant Style Targeting's target from {opponent} to {opponent, adjudicator}. Extend Appraise (§4 Step 1, Overwhelming tier already reveals 'a key individual's Belief') to reveal the adjudicator's primary Conviction/Resonant-Style, then allow a 'targeting adjudicator' declaration whose confirmed match lowers effective resistance (the adjudicator-facing analogue of the opponent-facing +1D/strain effects). No new resolver — the Targeting Effects table is reused with resistance-delta as the adjudicator-side payoff.

verdict.evidence_quote: Resonant Style Targeting — Targeting Declaration: "Before Argue roll (Step 3): player declares 'targeting Resonant Style.' GM confirms match. Requires: (a) argument uses Contest style mapping to the declared MS, (b) content addresses a known Belief, (c) player has previously discovered the MS." (params/contest_extensions.md §Resonant Style Targeting). The Targeting Effects table's payoffs ("+1D Argue", "NPC +2 strain", "NPC Belief revision Ob −1") are all opponent-NPC-facing; the adjudicator is never a declared target.
verdict.reasoning: The gap is real as described. The argument→Conviction dot-product (Resonant Style Targeting) is fully implemented and is exclusively aimed at the opponent NPC (params/contest_extensions.md §Resonant Style Targeting; npc_behavior_v30.md §1.3 "Resonant Style | The NPC is vulnerable to | ... | Contest mapping"). The adjudicator — the party who DECLARES the verdict — influences resolution only through three coarse channels, none of which is a tailored argument-meaning match against the adjudicator's individual Conviction: (1) a COLLECTIVE "audience boost" of +1D keyed to the dominant faction's Ethical Mode and one boosted axis (social_contest_v30.md §2 Step 3 boost table: Church/Divine Command/Obscuring, Crown/Virtue Ethics/Revealing, etc.) — this is a faction scalar, not the judge's Conviction; (2) the adjudicator TYPE choosing the Argue primary attribute (§3: Expert judge→Cognition, Crowd→Charisma); (3) audience resistance (avg faction Stability −1). The Overwhelming Appraise reveal ("a key individual's Belief") surfaces a Belief, not a targetable Resonant Style, and wires to no resolution bonus. I checked whether the gap is already-handled and it is NOT: (a) §9.4b "Adjudicator Thread Response" (ED-667) is a Certainty-indexed REACTION to Thread use, not argument-meaning targeting; (b) a real "Adjudicator Conviction → Panel Bias (+1D weight)" mechanic exists ONLY as a sim recommendation in tests/sim/sim_open_items_2026-04-16.md §ED-137 Model B and was never propagated to canon — the canonical ledger entry ED-137 (canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl line 137) resolves it as "Expert Judge (Cognition-primary) confirmed provisional. Full panel mechanics deferred to Contest System v3," and social_contest_v30.md still carries "[PROVISIONAL — ED-137: panel mechanics not yet designed]" (lines 37, 109, 588). So no shipped rule lets a player tailor an argument to the verdict-declaring adjudicator's own Conviction.
verdict.refined_improvement: Scope the fix to the Expert Judge adjudicator type, where the adjudicator IS a named NPC carrying a real primary Conviction / Resonant Style in npc_behavior_v30 §2 — for Crowd and No-adjudicator types the collective audience boost is correct by design (a crowd has no single judge to dot-product against), so do not touch those. For Expert-Judge contests: (1) extend the §4 Step 1 Overwhelming-Appraise reveal so it can surface the named adjudicator's primary Resonant Style (it already reveals "a key individual's Belief"); (2) add an "adjudicator" value to the Targeting Declaration's target set; (3) on a confirmed adjudicator-facing match, reuse the EXISTING Targeting Effects table with the payoff expressed as an audience-resistance delta (−1 effective resistance on the matched exchange) rather than opponent strain — no new resolver, no parallel subsystem, the same style→axis→Conviction dot-product already shipped. This is strictly the engine-disciplined reuse FG-3 proposes; the only correction is bounding it to the one adjudicator type that has a targetable individual, which also right-sizes leverage from high to medium (one type, with §9.4b adjudicator-reactivity and the deferred ED-137 panel-bias idea as partial precedent to build on).

gaps.title: No sanior-pars dynamic: the loser cannot contest the legitimacy of the verdict
gaps.research_basis: Politics model 9: the count was construed as DISCERNING the sanior et maior pars ('sounder and greater part'), and crucially the LOSER could claim to BE the sounder part — discernment-language weaponized inside the dispute. The verdict's legitimacy is itself contestable.
gaps.system_state: Post-contest resolution is terminal: the GM reveals the ledger, the Persuasion Track position dictates the outcome, and (for Decisive wins) a binding Obligation is named. The only re-litigation paths are Chain Contests (triggered only by Compromise) and Church-specific Parliamentary Stay/Acquittal.

locations.quote:
GM reveals ledger.
- Persuasion Track ≥ 7 = Side A wins; ≤ 3 = Side B wins; 4–6 = compromise (GM narrates partial outcome proportional to final position).
When a contest ends in Compromise (Persuasion Track 4–6), the tension is deferred, not resolved.

gaps.critique: The sanior-pars insight is that a count/verdict is not self-legitimating — the losing minority can assert it is the 'sounder part' and reopen the dispute on grounds of quality-over-quantity. Valoria's verdict is, by contrast, mechanically final the instant the track crosses a threshold (outside the Compromise→Chain and Church-Stay carve-outs). A DECISIVE loser has no in-system move to challenge the verdict's legitimacy — only to violate the resulting Obligation, which is framed purely as breach with penalties, not as a legitimacy claim. This omits exactly the dynamic the politics corpus says is the richest: the verdict as a contestable construal, not a settled fact.
gaps.churn_relevance: This is precisely the FAILURE-MODE-GENERATES-STORY churn the philosophy prizes (capture / holdout / the antechamber maneuver). A loser who can stake a sanior-pars claim — 'the crowd was swayed but the SOUND judgment is mine' — seeds schism, appeal, and counter-coalition arcs. Today a decisive loss is comparatively inert (Contest Fatigue, an Obligation), foreclosing high-value emergent narrative.
gaps.improvement: Add a Decisive-loss option 'Contest the Verdict (sanior-pars holdout)': reuse the Obligation clock bucket — instead of accepting the Obligation, the loser converts it into a Legitimacy clock seeded at the verdict margin. While that clock runs, the winner's Obligation is enforceable but the loser accrues a standing claim that resolves via a follow-up contest before a DIFFERENT adjudicator armature (FG-2/FG-3), where succeeding overturns/renegotiates the ruling. This reuses clocks + the adjudicator-armature kernel; no new subsystem. It also gives the existing 'No-adjudicator stall' (§2 Step 4) a principled adjudicated analogue.

verdict.evidence_quote: Player Obligations: When the player loses a contest, they receive an Obligation. The player is not forced to comply — they may violate it. Violation consequences apply: the player's faction loses Mandate, the player loses Reputation, and the NPC who won the original contest gains Disposition +1 toward the player's rivals (the violation justifies their position).
verdict.reasoning: The cited quotes are exact (§6 line 218; §6.3 line 298) and the system-state characterization is accurate. I grepped the full corpus for sanior/sounder/maior pars/legitimacy-contestation/overturn/reopen/re-litigate across designs/ and the whole repo: NO such mechanic exists. The only re-litigation paths are exactly the three the finding names — Chain Contests (§6.3, triggered ONLY by Compromise 4-6: "the tension is deferred, not resolved"), Parliamentary Stay / Heresy-Investigation closure (§7.3/§10.1, Church-specific), and Succession Contests (§7.2, triggered by leader removal, NOT by a loser challenging a verdict). For a DECISIVE loser (Track ≥7/≤3 in a non-Compromise, non-Church contest) the ONLY post-verdict move is to VIOLATE the resulting Obligation (§6.1 Player Obligations, evidence quote above), framed purely as breach-with-penalties, never as a quality-over-quantity legitimacy claim. The "Holdout" matches I found are unrelated (Edeyja's NPC arc name in npc_behavior; settlement-resistance in settlement_layer §6.3). The narrative_scenario_chains "legitimacy claim" hit is a specific Baralta-vs-Church Thread-knowledge arc, not a generalized verdict-contestation primitive. So the gap is real as described. The improvement is engine-disciplined: §6.1 already states Obligations "are tracked as clocks per clock_registry_v30" (line 282) and the distillation_coherence_report establishes the contest IS the sigma kernel / adjudicator armature — so converting the Obligation clock into a legitimacy-holdout clock that resolves before a different adjudicator armature reuses both primitives with no bolt-on subsystem. The churn payoff (schism/appeal/counter-coalition arcs from a sanior-pars holdout) is genuinely high-value and currently unreachable for decisive losses, consistent with the world-player-churn axiom that flags inert outcomes. I hold leverage at medium (not high): this is one missing branch on an already heavily-elaborated resolution stage (Obligations, Wagers, Chain, Succession, Tribunal, Stay), and the existing Compromise→Chain path already covers the most common non-decisive case; the gap is specifically the decisive-loss holdout.
verdict.refined_improvement: Add a Decisive-loss option "Contest the Verdict (sanior-pars holdout)" that reuses existing plumbing rather than introducing a new clock type. On a Decisive loss, instead of accepting the Obligation, the loser may convert the SAME Obligation clock (already "tracked as clocks per clock_registry_v30", §6.1 line 282) into a standing Legitimacy claim seeded at the verdict margin. While it runs, the winner's Obligation is fully enforceable, but the holdout accrues a claim that resolves via a follow-up contest before a DIFFERENT adjudicator armature — reusing the §7.2 Succession-Contest pattern (already the canon precedent for "a different institutional body re-adjudicates a question") and the §6.3 mechanism that routes deferred tension into a player_agency §4.2 Scene Slate entry. Succeeding overturns/renegotiates the ruling; failing converts the standing claim into an aggravated Obligation. This adds no new subsystem (sigma resolver + clock bucket + Scene Slate only) and gives the §2 Step-4 "no-adjudicator stall" a principled adjudicated analogue. Constrain availability so it does not undermine the deliberate finality the spec built for Church proceedings (the Excommunication-Tribunal "correct counter is preventing the filing" design intent in §7.1).

gaps.title: Panel adjudicator still undesigned (ED-137) — the one type whose research is explicitly about plural deliberation
gaps.research_basis: Politics model 9's sanior-et-maior-pars is fundamentally a PANEL/collective-discernment construct (a body discerning the sounder part among many judges). The Panel type is therefore the adjudicator that most directly carries the research's deliberative content — and it is the one left unbuilt.
gaps.system_state: Panel is listed as a type but punts to Expert Judge as a provisional placeholder, with mechanics flagged unbuilt since ED-137.

locations.quote: | Panel | Multiple individual judges deliberating | [EDITORIAL: ED-137 — panel mechanics not yet designed. Use Expert Judge as provisional.] | Cognition |
| ED-137 | Panel adjudicator type not yet designed. Using Expert Judge as provisional. | P2 |

gaps.critique: Collapsing 'multiple individual judges deliberating' into a single Cognition Expert Judge erases the only structurally PLURAL adjudicator — the exact construct the politics corpus models (a body discerning the sounder part). With the FG-2 armature in hand, Panel is not a hard new subsystem: it is a SET of adjudicator armatures rather than one. The provisional Expert-Judge stand-in throws away the cross-judge disagreement that is the entire point of a panel and the natural home for a sanior-pars (FG-4) contest.
gaps.churn_relevance: A panel of differing armatures is a churn factory: an argument can split the panel (win the Precedent judges, lose the Faith judge), producing fractional/contested verdicts and minority opinions that seed follow-on play — far richer than a scalar pass/fail. Leaving it as Expert-Judge suppresses that emergent disagreement.
gaps.improvement: Design Panel directly on FG-2: a Panel is N adjudicator armatures. Resolve by dot-producting each exchange's argument against EACH panelist armature (the §5 composition + dot-product already exists), count panelists moved past their individual resistance, and let the panel verdict EMERGE from the per-judge tally (a near-literal sanior/maior-pars count). Split panels feed FG-4's legitimacy contest. This is purely a fan-out over the existing single-adjudicator kernel — no new primitive.

verdict.reasoning:
The cited quotes are accurate: in the LIVE canonical doc (social_contest_v30.md §2 Step 1, §3 table, §12 ED-137) Panel is genuinely provisional and punts to Expert Judge. But FG-5 frames this as "the one type left UNBUILT" needing fresh design, and that is wrong on two counts.

(1) A built, validated Panel model already exists in the groundup engine. designs/audit/2026-06-03-contest-groundup/contract.py:37-51 defines `class Panel(members: tuple)` that aggregates per-member learned/hostile/discipline/character — exactly "N adjudicators, verdict emerges from a per-member tally." modes.py:60-72 wires a `deliberative_body_venue` where "a Panel casts a terminal vote with per-juror variance (VoteAtClose)... the vote can cross the room" — i.e. the split-panel / cross-judge disagreement FG-5 says is "thrown away" is precisely what the built model produces. The comprehensive audit names this directly (ANALYSIS.md:205, P3-16): "Canon's Panel adjudicator is provisional ('use Expert Judge'); the groundup engine has a built, validated Panel model (member character / discipline / learned-hostile aggregation). Propagation closes ED-137 for free." So the work FG-5 proposes is a PROPAGATION/commit of an existing model, not a design task.

(2) FG-5's prescribed mechanism misattributes the primitive. It says build Panel "directly on FG-2: a Panel is N adjudicator armatures... dot-product each argument against EACH panelist armature." But the distillation report (distillation_coherence_report.md:41,62) is explicit that the armature dot-product is the INTERPRETATION primitive, NOT the contest resolver ("`armature_dot_product` is not a resolver... it's primitive #2, not a module resolver class"). The contest adjudicator runs on the SIGMA resolver with a flat scalar resistance (avg faction Stability, §2 Step 4) plus an attribute selector (§3 table) — there is no adjudicator armature in the live engine. The built Panel correctly fans out over the sigma kernel (per-member readiness/character/vote), not over conviction armatures. FG-5 would bolt the wrong primitive onto the wrong layer.

Net: the gap in the live doc is real, but the deliberative-plural mechanic FG-5 wants already exists and is validated; the leverage and effort are both overstated (it is a low-effort propagation, not a medium-effort new design), and the proposed armature-dot-product mechanism violates engine discipline.
verdict.evidence_quote: **P3-16 — Convergence, not defect: ED-137 Panel.** Canon's Panel adjudicator is provisional ("use Expert Judge"); the groundup engine has a built, validated Panel model (member character / discipline / learned-hostile aggregation). Propagation closes ED-137 for free.
verdict.refined_improvement: Do not design Panel anew, and do not attach it to the FG-2 conviction-armature dot-product (that is the interpretation primitive, not the contest resolver — distillation_coherence_report.md:41/62). Instead PROPAGATE the already-built, validated groundup Panel (contract.py `class Panel` aggregating per-member learned/hostile/discipline/character; modes.py `deliberative_body_venue` with VoteAtClose per-juror variance) into the canonical social_contest_v30.md, resolving ED-137. This keeps Panel on the existing sigma resolver (per-member fan-out over readiness/character, terminal vote that can cross the room), preserves the cross-judge split that FG-5 rightly wants, and lets a split panel feed the FG-4 legitimacy contest. This is a commit/propagation task, not a new subsystem — exactly as ANALYSIS.md P3-16 states ("closes ED-137 for free"). Watch the documented edge: contract.py:42-44 Panel tie resolves to not-learned/not-hostile; pick a deliberate tie-break rule on propagation.

gaps.title: The named shady styles (Suppression, Insinuation) are pure-upside dice options, not sanctionable tactics
gaps.research_basis: Pragma-dialectics VI.2: healthiest when treated LEAST as a contest to win; eristic is the degenerate type.
gaps.system_state: An Obscuring win places a Doubt Marker on the OPPONENT at no cost; the marker has no self-cost.

locations.quote: Suppression | Memory + Obscuring | Burying inconvenient history
Doubt Marker effect: opponent's next winning exchange has its margin reduced by 2 before resistance is applied (minimum 0).

gaps.critique: The design LABELS these dirty yet makes them strictly dominant where the audience boosts Obscuring (Church): +1D plus a free debuff, never costing the obscurer. Eristic as optimal play.
gaps.churn_relevance: No Obscuring backfire means no caught-in-a-lie story seed.
gaps.improvement: On an Obscuring loss or successful opponent Appraise, redirect the Doubt Marker onto the OBSCURER and add 1 strain (provisional CR5 self-Face-backfire shape, designs/audit/2026-06-01); propagate it into canon.

verdict.evidence_quote: Obscuring win: no track movement; place a Doubt Marker on the opponent (−2 to opponent's next winning margin; one active at a time; consumed on use).
verdict.reasoning:
The critique's system-state description is accurate against the LIVE corpus. params/contest.md:40,42 define Suppression/Insinuation as Memory/Projection + Obscuring; line 55 gives Church a +1D Obscuring boost; line 95 and social_contest_v30.md:179-183 define the Obscuring win as: no track movement, place a Doubt Marker on the OPPONENT (−2 to their next winning margin), with NO self-cost, NO strain, NO failure-redirect. I grepped social_contest_v30.md, contest.md, contest_extensions.md, npc_behavior_v30.md, and sim/personal/contest.py for backfire / self-cost / caught-in-a-lie / Face-redirect: none exists in the shipped system (the sim does not even model the Doubt Marker). So the eristic-with-no-downside gap and the missing caught-in-a-lie churn seed are real.

Crucially, the EXACT fix the finding proposes is ALREADY DESIGNED but unpropagated. designs/audit/2026-06-01-contest-redesign/RATIFIED_2026-06-01.md CR5 ratified (provisionally, by the owner): "Orientations re-grounded: Direct -> moves Persuasion (merits); Indirect -> attacks opponent Face (ethos), with self-Face backfire on failure ... F7 self-gating: obstruction is bounded by your own standing (Quintilian/Nyaya)." Its omega block even cites Conviction Scars as the consequence anchor. But that doc's own "Propagation follow-through (not yet applied)" section (lines 22-30) confirms the rewrite never reached social_contest_v30.md or params/contest.md — and my grep confirms zero Face/sigma/self-Face/Suspicion tokens in the live files (the only "Face" hits are unrelated: "face Disposition penalty", "face new filings"). So canon is in a known-inconsistent state: the dirty styles ship as pure-upside dice options while the self-gating fix sits ratified-but-stranded in the audit folder.

One correction to severity framing: the finding calls Obscuring "strictly dominant." It is not strictly dominant — an Obscuring win forgoes Persuasion-Track movement (the merits clock) in exchange for the denial marker, so it is a tempo/denial trade, not free value on the winning axis. But the load-bearing claim (no self-cost, no backfire, hence no churn seed) is exactly correct, and leverage stays high because the canonical remedy is already authored and merely needs propagation — low effort, high coherence payoff.
verdict.refined_improvement: Do NOT add a new bolt-on backfire rule. Instead, propagate the already-ratified CR5 (designs/audit/2026-06-01-contest-redesign/RATIFIED_2026-06-01.md) into live canon: replace the pure-upside Doubt Marker in social_contest_v30.md §4 (lines 179-183) and params/contest.md (line 95) with the Indirect/Obscuring = attack-opponent-Face mechanic that carries self-Face backfire on failure (and, per the finding, redirect the marker onto the obscurer + 1 strain when the opponent's Appraise succeeds — contest_extensions.md §Appraise Revelation already supplies the Appraise hook to detect Obscuring). This is the engine-disciplined path: it uses the contest's own Face/standing track (CR3) and the sigma resolver rather than inventing a subsystem, and its consequence wires into Conviction Scars (npc_behavior_v30.md S3.3/3.4), which the CR5 omega block already names. Pair the propagation with an ED entry (CR1-CR7 ED-IDs are still unassigned per the audit doc) so the params/doc Doubt-Marker references are updated in one source-of-truth pass.

gaps.title: No general fouls layer: S9.4b is the only foul concept and covers only Thread use
gaps.research_basis: Pragma-dialectics (V.10): rules are constitutive. Walton-Krabbe: an unmarked mid-stream shift is bad-faith.
gaps.system_state: Recall rewards a verified citation but nothing penalizes a fabricated one; a genre dodge is a no-strain CROSS; the Sanction Suspicion Token is placed regardless of outcome but never defined.

locations.quote: Recall bonus: +2D when citing a specific, named, verifiable claim. Binary.
Sanction | ... | +1D Argue. Suspicion Token placed regardless of outcome.

gaps.critique: The foul-mirror of Recall is absent; the genre-dodge is rewarded; the Suspicion Token is inert and collides with the Doubt Marker rename.
gaps.churn_relevance: An uncaught fabricated citation or a caught dodge feeds the 6.3 Chain Contest / Scene Slate.
gaps.improvement: Generalize S9.4b into an Adjudicator Foul-Response table; use Appraise as the detection roll; define the Suspicion Token as the obscurer's-own foul-mark. No new resolver.

verdict.evidence_quote: No action in Part 6 operative system to contest a Memory bonus claim (Challenge exists in deprecated quaestio only). **Severity:** P2 — play culture dependency; incentivises unverifiable claims.
verdict.reasoning: The core gap is REAL: social_contest_v30.md:144 grants Recall +2D "when citing a specific, named, verifiable claim" with no verification roll and no foul-mirror for fabrication; the only operative foul rules are §9.4b (Thread use) and §9.6 Chamber Violence (physical=forfeit). A prior sim audit independently logged exactly this hole — tests/sim/sim_social_contest_stress.md:161-162: "No action in Part 6 operative system to contest a Memory bonus claim... incentivises unverifiable claims (P2)" — so it is NOT already-handled. The genre-dodge sub-claim also holds: CROSS (different genres) deals "No strain dealt" (social_contest_v30.md:171), so answering off-genre to dodge a CLASH costs zero. The Suspicion Token sub-claim holds and is sharper than stated: contest_extensions.md:47 places a "Suspicion Token... regardless of outcome" that is defined NOWHERE; ED-897 in shipped canon made "Doubt Marker" canonical (params/contest.md:95, social_contest_v30.md:180-183) while the 2026-05-17 decision (designs/audit/2026-05-17-scene-combat-contest/decisions.md:67) renamed the OPPOSITE direction, orphaning the term — the audit workflow (tools/observability/social_contest_audit.workflow.js:89) already flags "Doubt Marker vs Suspicion Token (same mechanic, two names?)". WHY OVERSTATED: the title's claim that "§9.4b is the only foul concept" is false. §9.6 Chamber Violence is a second foul-with-penalty, and the Doubt Marker penalizes the Obscuring-win move-shape (social_contest_v30.md:180).
